B12.history: B12 was founded in 2015 in New York as an AI-powered website builder combining machine learning with human designers. No notable ownership changes documented.
B12.summary_abuse: B12 is an AI website builder. Like all website builders it could be used to create fraudulent websites, but B12 applies human review to designs which reduces the risk compared to fully automated alternatives. No specific documented abuse incidents.

B2C Europe.history: B2C Europe was founded in 2000 in the Netherlands by Rijk Van Meekeren and Jose Vega as the first independent logistics service provider for cross-border parcel delivery in Europe. The company served major e-commerce clients including Amazon, eBay, and L'Oreal. In August 2021, Maersk acquired B2C Europe for approximately $86 million. The B2C Europe brand was retired on June 1, 2022, when the business was rebranded as Maersk E-Delivery.
B2C Europe.summary_abuse: B2C Europe was a legitimate B2B logistics service provider with no documented abuse incidents. The company served established e-commerce platforms and had no consumer-facing components that could be exploited for phishing or fraud. Following its acquisition by Maersk in 2021 and rebranding to Maersk E-Delivery in 2022, the B2C Europe brand no longer exists.

B2Chat.history: B2Chat is a Colombian multi-channel customer support platform integrating WhatsApp, Facebook, Instagram, Telegram, and live chat. No notable ownership changes documented.
B2Chat.summary_abuse: B2Chat has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B multi-channel messaging platform with no consumer-facing content hosting that would attract fraudsters.

Babel.history: Babel was created by Sebastian McKenzie in 2014, originally named 6to5, and renamed Babel in 2015. It became the de facto JavaScript transpiler, enabling developers to use modern ES2015+ syntax on older runtimes. It is maintained by a volunteer team under the OpenJS Foundation umbrella and has been central to the JavaScript toolchain for a decade.
Babel.summary_abuse: Babel has no notable abuse signal. It is a backend build-tool and developer-side transcompiler with no consumer-facing surface, no hosting, and no runtime network component that fraudsters could target or exploit.

Bablic.history: Bablic is an Israeli website localization and translation platform founded around 2013. No notable ownership changes documented.
Bablic.summary_abuse: Bablic has no notable abuse signal. It is a website translation service with no consumer-facing content hosting that would attract fraudsters.

Babylist.history: Babylist was founded in 2011 as a universal baby registry platform allowing parents to add gifts from any store. It has grown into one of the largest baby registry platforms in the US, backed by venture capital.
Babylist.summary_abuse: Babylist has no notable abuse signal as a platform. It is a consumer baby registry service. Standard e-commerce fraud patterns (fake accounts, return fraud) apply as with any retail platform, but Babylist is not specifically targeted or exploited by fraudsters at a notable rate.

Babylon.js.history: Created by David Catuhe at Microsoft in 2013 as a side project, Babylon.js grew into a full-time Microsoft team and is now one of the leading WebGL and WebGPU 3D rendering engines. It was initially released under the Microsoft Public License and later relicensed to Apache 2.0. The project has accumulated over 500 contributors and 47,000 commits, with 627 releases as of 2026. It was used by Ubisoft for an Assassin's Creed title as early as 2014. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events have occurred.
Babylon.js.summary_abuse: Babylon.js has no notable abuse signal. It is a client-side 3D rendering library with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters target. It cannot be used for phishing hosting, spam delivery, malware C2, or similar abuse patterns as it provides only WebGL/WebGPU graphics rendering APIs with no network proxying, URL redirection, file hosting, or messaging features. No documented incidents of significant abusive use exist. There are no anonymous signup or free-tier vectors since it is a self-hosted client library.

Back In Stock.history: Launched on the Shopify App Store in May 2011 as a back-in-stock alert app. Acquired by Amp (Use Amp Pte Ltd) in 2022, a Singapore-based commerce platform founded by serial entrepreneurs Patrick Barnes and Cameron Priest (former TradeGecko co-founders whose prior company exited to Intuit in 2020). The app now has over 112,000 Shopify installs and is part of Amp's suite alongside Lifetimely, Slide Cart, and Bundles.
Back In Stock.summary_abuse: Back in Stock has minimal abuse signal. It is a Shopify back-in-stock alert app that does not host arbitrary user content, offer custom domain hosting, or enable anonymous signup. Customer data collection is limited to email and phone for restock notifications, triggered by merchant inventory changes. The app operates exclusively through authenticated Shopify stores with established merchant accounts. Amp publishes an Acceptable Use Policy under its Terms of Service and accepts abuse reports via hello@useamp.com. No notable public incidents of abuse have been documented for this app.

Backbase.history: Founded in 2003 by Jouk Pleiter in Amsterdam, Netherlands. Privately held with $128.6M in venture funding. Serves 120+ banks globally as an AI-native banking operating system. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events.
Backbase.summary_abuse: Backbase is an enterprise banking platform deployed by financial institutions. It has no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters directly target; abuse would require compromising a bank's deployment. The company publishes a responsible disclosure policy (security@backbase.com with PGP) and maintains SOC 2 Type 2 certification. No notable public incidents of platform-level abuse documented.

Backbone.js.history: Created in 2010 by Jeremy Ashkenas at DocumentCloud as one of the first JavaScript MVC frameworks, popularized single-page application architecture. Widely adopted in the early 2010s by Airbnb, Trello, Hulu, USA Today, and others. Maintenance transitioned from Ashkenas to Julian Gonggrijp around 2022. Largely superseded by React, Vue, and Angular since the mid-2010s, but still receives releases (v1.6.1, April 2025). No deprecation or ownership change events.
Backbone.js.summary_abuse: Backbone.js is a client-side JavaScript library with no consumer-facing surface area and no documented abuse patterns. It is not a hosted service, offers no free tier with custom-domain support, and does not expose file-hosting, URL-shortening, or mass-email features that fraudsters target. The library is a runtime dependency loaded in the browser; abuse risk is negligible.

Backdrop.history: Backdrop CMS was created in 2013 as a fork of Drupal 7 by Nate Lampton and other developers concerned about the migration complexity of Drupal 8. Version 1.0 was released on January 15, 2015 after 16 months of development. The project is stewarded by the Software Freedom Conservancy, a non-profit foundation. It has maintained a consistent 4-month release cycle since launch with no notable ownership changes, forks, or deprecation events.
Backdrop.summary_abuse: Backdrop CMS has no notable abuse signal beyond what any self-hosted PHP content management system would face. As server-side software installed on a site operator's own infrastructure, abuse risk depends on hosting configuration and site content rather than the software itself. The project maintains a security team (security@backdropcms.org) and follows a responsible disclosure process, managing security issues privately until resolution. No notable public incidents of abuse specific to Backdrop have been documented, and no bug bounty program exists.

BackerKit.history: Founded in 2012 by Maxwell Salzberg and Rosanna Yau as a Y Combinator-backed pledge management platform. Acquired Crowd Ox in 2020 and launched its own crowdfunding platform in 2022, expanding from a Kickstarter/Indiegogo add-on into a full end-to-end crowdfunding platform. No major ownership changes or controversies documented since.
BackerKit.summary_abuse: BackerKit is a crowdfunding and pledge management platform with a low-to-moderate abuse risk profile. The primary abuse vector is fraudulent or misleading creator projects that fail to deliver rewards. BackerKit mitigates this with a dedicated Trust & Safety team, human review of every project before launch, automated monitoring tools, and a reporting system for backers. The platform publishes clear content guidelines and maintains a trustandsafety@backerkit.com contact. No major public abuse incidents or security breaches have been documented. Chargebacks are rare and handled through an established dispute process. The platform does not offer anonymous signup for creators and requires payment credentials through Stripe.

Backstage.history: Backstage (yourbackstage.io) is a provider of pre-made marketing templates (emails, videos, funnels) for affiliate marketers. Limited public history available.
Backstage.summary_abuse: Backstage provides ready-made affiliate marketing funnel and email templates. The affiliate marketing space is frequently associated with misleading or exaggerated claims, fake testimonials, and spam campaigns. Tools that automate and lower barriers to affiliate campaigns carry elevated potential for facilitating spam or misleading marketing materials.

Backstretch.history: Created by Scott Robbin in 2009 as a jQuery plugin for dynamically-resized, slideshow-capable background images. It became a widely-used jQuery utility hosted on cdnjs and npm. Around 2020, maintainership briefly transferred to community contributor danielgindi who merged a fork, but the project has been looking for active maintainers since then and has seen no code changes since August 2020.
Backstretch.summary_abuse: Backstretch is a client-side jQuery plugin for background images with no user-facing content hosting, no server-side component, no accounts or data collection, and no surface for abuse. It has no documented abuse signal.

Bag.history: Founded around 2020 by Yallaweb Software as an Arabic-language hosted ecommerce platform targeting the Egyptian and broader Arab market. No notable ownership changes, forks, or deprecation events since launch.
Bag.summary_abuse: Bag (Shanta) is an Egyptian-hosted ecommerce SaaS serving 10,000+ Arabic-language stores. As a hosted platform with custom-domain support, trial-based signup, and integrated Facebook/TikTok ad tracking, it carries the standard abuse risk of regional ecommerce platforms: potential for fake storefronts and dropshipping scams. The vendor publishes a terms of service and privacy policy but no dedicated abuse-reporting channel or published AUP was found on the public site. No notable public abuse incidents or security research reports were identified for this platform.

Bagisto.history: Bagisto was launched on October 31, 2018 by Webkul, an India-based ecommerce software company with deep roots in platforms like Magento and OpenCart. It was created as a modern, MIT-licensed ecommerce framework built on Laravel and Vue.js to give developers a free, extensible alternative to heavier commercial platforms. The project has grown steadily to over 26,000 GitHub stars and remains actively maintained by Webkul.
Bagisto.summary_abuse: Bagisto is a self-hosted open-source ecommerce framework with no notable abuse signal. Because it is deployed on merchant-owned infrastructure rather than a shared hosting service, it does not offer attackers a common platform surface like free-tier SaaS tools do. Abuse risk is analogous to other self-hosted ecommerce software: a compromised or poorly secured Bagisto instance could be used to run an illicit storefront, but this is an operator misconfiguration issue rather than a platform-level abuse vector. Webkul publishes usage terms and actively maintains the project, and there are no documented reports of Bagisto being widely exploited as a fraud or phishing platform.

Bahooosh.history: Bahooosh is an Iranian online store builder developed by Parnas Creative Agency, launching around 2023. The platform targets the Persian-speaking ecommerce market with AI-powered site building, inventory management, and SEO tools. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events since launch.
Bahooosh.summary_abuse: Bahooosh has minimal abuse signal. As a Persian-language SaaS store builder serving Iranian merchants, it has no documented international abuse patterns. The platform offers custom domain support and a free tier, which theoretically enables scam storefronts, but its small market footprint and Iranian regulatory context limit abuse exposure. No notable security incidents or abuse reports have been publicly documented.

Baidu Analytics (百度统计).history: Baidu Analytics (百度统计) is a free web analytics service provided by Baidu, China's largest search engine. It launched around 2010 as a direct counterpart to Google Analytics for Chinese websites. It is a subsidiary product of Baidu Inc.
Baidu Analytics (百度统计).summary_abuse: Baidu Analytics is the dominant web analytics tool for Chinese websites. It sends user data to Baidu servers in China, which raises significant data sovereignty concerns for non-Chinese operators. It is not GDPR compliant and cannot be used lawfully on sites serving EU users without additional measures. There are no specific fraud or phishing abuse patterns attributable to the analytics tag itself, but the data collection practices are a meaningful privacy concern.

Baidu Maps.history: Launched in 2005 by Baidu, it is the dominant web mapping service in China. It expanded to include 3D views in 2010 and street view (Panorama) in 2013, and has since added global map coverage using data from various providers.
Baidu Maps.summary_abuse: Baidu Maps is a major mapping service and API. While its API could theoretically be embedded in fraudulent applications, it is overwhelmingly used for legitimate navigation and location services. It has no notable consumer-facing abuse surface.

Baidu Search Box.history: Baidu, founded in 2000 by Robin Li and Eric Xu and headquartered in Beijing, China, is the dominant Chinese-language search engine. The Baidu Search Box is an embeddable widget introduced to let webmasters embed Baidu search functionality directly on their pages. Baidu went public on Nasdaq in 2005 and remains China's leading search platform.
Baidu Search Box.summary_abuse: Baidu's search widget has been observed in the context of search spam and SEO manipulation schemes targeting Chinese-language audiences. The widget itself is relatively benign, but Baidu's ad network and ecosystem have historically been associated with scam ads and fraudulent listings. Baidu has faced regulatory scrutiny in China over misleading medical and financial advertisements. The widget is rarely seen on abuse infrastructure.

Balance.history: Balance was founded in 2020 by Bar Geron and Yoni Shuster in San Francisco, California. The company provides financial infrastructure for B2B commerce, offering embedded payments, net terms financing, and digital checkout for business-to-business merchants and marketplaces. The company raised $56M in a 2022 Series B round led by Forerunner Ventures with participation from Ribbit Capital and Stripe.
Balance.summary_abuse: Balance operates in the B2B payments space and processes business transactions, making it a lower consumer-facing abuse risk than retail payment providers. As a payment infrastructure provider, Balance is subject to standard financial regulation and KYC/AML requirements. No notable public abuse incidents have been documented.

BambooHR.history: BambooHR was founded in 2008 by Ben Peterson and Ryan Sanders in Lindon, Utah. The founders bootstrapped the company for its first four years, forgoing salaries during early development. BambooHR grew into one of the leading HR software platforms for small and mid-size businesses, serving over 34,000 customers across 190 countries. The company remained privately held and independent.
BambooHR.summary_abuse: BambooHR handles sensitive employee HR data including personal information, payroll details, and benefits. The platform is a legitimate HR SaaS vendor with no notable history of abuse or fraud. The main risk is data breach exposure given the sensitive nature of the employee data processed. BambooHR publishes a clear privacy policy and maintains SOC 2 Type 2 compliance.

Bambuser.history: Bambuser was founded in 2007 in Stockholm, Sweden, initially as a mobile live-streaming platform for citizen journalism. The company pivoted to video commerce in 2019, reorienting around live video shopping and shoppable video for brands and retailers. The pivot attracted over $100 million in investment. Bambuser is publicly listed and serves brands including LVMH group companies, Samsung, and Tommy Hilfiger.
Bambuser.summary_abuse: Bambuser is a legitimate video commerce platform focused on enterprise retail clients. Its closed platform model with enterprise contracts limits exposure to abuse. No notable incidents of the platform being used for fraud or scams have been documented. Live shopping streams are moderated by the brand clients.

Banana Splash.history: Banana Splash is a mobile website personalization and conversion optimization tool built by a team in Tel Aviv with backgrounds in online marketing. The platform analyzes mobile visitor sessions in real-time to trigger targeted calls to action. The product targets small to mid-size e-commerce businesses and appears to have limited recent development activity.
Banana Splash.summary_abuse: Banana Splash is a small mobile marketing tool with no notable abuse history. The platform collects behavioral tracking data from mobile visitors, which carries normal privacy considerations. No documented cases of the platform being used for malicious purposes.

BandsInTown Events Widget.history: Bandsintown was founded around 2008 as a concert discovery and event promotion platform for musicians and music fans. The Events Widget is a free embeddable tool that lets musicians display their upcoming tour dates on any website. The platform grew to serve hundreds of millions of fans and hundreds of thousands of artists.
BandsInTown Events Widget.summary_abuse: The Bandsintown Events Widget is a straightforward event display tool for musicians with no notable abuse history. The widget embeds event data from the Bandsintown platform onto third-party sites and carries minimal privacy risk. The platform is used legitimately by artists worldwide.

Bandwango.history: Bandwango was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Murray, Utah. The company provides the Destination Experience Engine (DXE), a B2B SaaS platform for destination marketing organizations and convention and visitor bureaus. The company raised $3.12 million in a private placement with investors including Next Frontier Capital.
Bandwango.summary_abuse: Bandwango is a niche travel technology platform serving destination marketing organizations and operates in a low-abuse-risk segment. The platform manages digital visitor passes and local experience marketplaces. No documented abuse or fraud incidents have been identified.

BannerBoo.history: BannerBoo is an online HTML5 and animated banner maker SaaS created by a design and marketing team. The platform offers a drag-and-drop editor with over 12,000 templates for creating animated ads, GIFs, and social media banners without coding skills. It launched with a freemium model offering paid plans from $14.99 per month.
BannerBoo.summary_abuse: BannerBoo is a banner design tool with no notable abuse history. The platform could theoretically be used to create deceptive or misleading banner advertisements, but there are no documented cases of systematic abuse. The service operates as a standard design tool with a legitimate paying user base.

Banno Banking.history: Banno was founded in Iowa as a digital banking engagement platform for community banks and credit unions. Jack Henry and Associates acquired Banno and integrated it into their fintech product suite. The Banno platform has grown to serve over 10.5 million registered users across hundreds of community financial institutions in the United States.
Banno Banking.summary_abuse: Banno Banking operates in the regulated financial services sector under Jack Henry and Associates, a publicly traded company. As a digital banking platform for community banks and credit unions, it is subject to banking regulations and oversight. There is no notable history of fraud or abuse associated with the platform. The platform is used by legitimate financial institutions.

Banshee.history: Banshee was originally named Sonance and was created in early 2005 by Aaron Bockover. It was renamed Banshee in 2005 and was built on Mono and Gtk# using the GStreamer multimedia framework. It was briefly the default music player in Ubuntu and Linux Mint. Development effectively ended with version 2.6.2 released in February 2014, after which the project was abandoned. Banshee is strongly affiliated with the GNOME project.
Banshee.summary_abuse: Banshee is a defunct open-source desktop media player with no abuse history. As a local application with no server component or hosted service, it has no meaningful abuse surface.

Bant.history: Bant.io is a B2B lead generation and prospecting automation platform. No notable ownership changes documented.
Bant.summary_abuse: Bant is a B2B outreach automation tool. Such tools are regularly used for high-volume cold email campaigns which can constitute spam under anti-spam regulations. No specific documented abuse incidents, but the tool's core functionality of automating prospect outreach at scale warrants a moderate abuse score.

Barba.js.history: Barba.js is an open-source JavaScript library for creating smooth, fluid page transitions on websites by using AJAX to fetch page content and replacing only necessary DOM sections instead of full page reloads. It was originally created around 2014 and has been maintained by the open-source community. The GitHub repository under barbajs organization shows ongoing community contributions.
Barba.js.summary_abuse: Barba.js is a client-side JavaScript library for page transitions with no server component. It presents no notable abuse surface and has no documented misuse cases.

Baremetrics.history: Baremetrics was founded in 2013 as a subscription analytics tool for Stripe users. In November 2020, founder Josh Pigford sold the company to private equity firm Xenon Partners for approximately $4 million. The company continues to operate under PE ownership.
Baremetrics.summary_abuse: Baremetrics is a subscription analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. It integrates with payment processors to surface revenue metrics for SaaS businesses and has no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraud or phishing activity.

Barilliance.history: Barilliance was founded in 2009 in Tel Aviv, Israel. It provides AI-powered ecommerce personalization including cart abandonment emails, behavioral targeting, and product recommendations primarily for mid-market and enterprise online retailers. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are recorded.
Barilliance.summary_abuse: Barilliance is a B2B ecommerce personalization platform with no notable abuse signal. It is deployed by online retailers to deliver personalized content, and has no direct consumer-facing surface exploited by fraud actors.

Barion.history: Barion was founded around 2013 in Budapest, Hungary as an e-wallet and payment gateway authorized by the Hungarian Central Bank. It raised EUR 2 million in a Series B round in 2020 to expand across Europe. The company operates as an electronic money institution passported across 16 EEA countries.
Barion.summary_abuse: Barion is a regulated payment gateway operating under Hungarian central bank supervision with GDPR and PCI DSS obligations. As a payment processor, it carries inherent fraud risk from unauthorized card use, but the regulated institutional framework and merchant onboarding controls mitigate this. No specific public abuse incidents are recorded.

Base.history: Base (thebase.com) was founded in 2012 in Tokyo, Japan by Kazuma Ieiri, inspired by Shopify. It enables anyone to launch an online store for free with integrated payments through its subsidiary Pay, Inc. The company, BASE Inc., is publicly listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.
Base.summary_abuse: Base is a Japanese e-commerce platform primarily used by small and independent sellers. Its free tier with storefronts could theoretically be used for scam storefronts targeting Japanese consumers, but the platform requires identity verification for payment processing. No notable public abuse incidents are recorded.

Base UI.history: Base UI is an unstyled React component library developed by MUI, the creators of Material UI. Originally extracted from Material UI as headless components, Base UI was released as a standalone library. Version 1.0 was released in early 2026, marking production-ready stability after two years of development. The project is backed by MUI and benefits from contributors from the Radix and Floating UI communities.
Base UI.summary_abuse: Base UI is a headless React component library with no hosted service or server component. It presents no abuse surface and is used exclusively in client-side web development.

Base44.history: Base44 is an AI-powered no-code app builder launched in late 2024 that allows users to create full-stack web applications from natural language descriptions. The platform went viral shortly after launch, enabling non-technical users to generate complete apps including backend, database, authentication, and UI. Paid plans start at $16 per month.
Base44.summary_abuse: Base44 is an AI-powered app builder that enables rapid creation of web applications from text prompts. As a platform enabling anyone to spin up web apps quickly, there is potential for it to be used to create phishing pages, fake storefronts, or other deceptive applications, though no specific incidents have been documented. The platform is new and its abuse posture is not yet established.

BaseKit.history: BaseKit was founded in 2008 in Bristol, UK. It provides a white-label website builder and e-commerce platform distributed through telecom operators, hosting companies, and domain registrars. The company achieved B Corp certification. It serves over 100 reseller partners in 40 markets.
BaseKit.summary_abuse: BaseKit is a white-label website builder sold through telecoms and hosting providers. Its white-label distribution model means end-user quality depends heavily on reseller controls. Free or low-cost hosted sites under reseller domains could be used for phishing or fake storefronts, though no major public abuse campaigns have been attributed specifically to BaseKit infrastructure.

Basic.history: BASIC (Beginner's All-purpose Symbolic Instruction Code) was created in 1964 by John G. Kemeny and Thomas E. Kurtz at Dartmouth College. It was designed to make programming accessible to non-science students. Microsoft BASIC, developed by Bill Gates and Paul Allen in 1975, brought BASIC to home computers. Numerous dialects including GW-BASIC, QuickBASIC, and Visual Basic have been developed. Visual Basic remains in use today as a legacy enterprise language.
Basic.summary_abuse: BASIC is a foundational programming language with no meaningful abuse surface as a language specification. Compiled or interpreted BASIC programs can be misused like any executable code, but the language itself is not associated with abuse patterns.

Basil.css.history: Basil.css is a small open-source CSS framework hosted on GitHub under the Basilcss organization. It is designed as a lightweight and customizable UI framework to bridge the gap between developers who prefer CSS frameworks and those who prefer hand-crafted styling. The project has minimal community adoption and limited recent activity.
Basil.css.summary_abuse: Basil.css is a niche client-side CSS framework with no hosted service. It presents no abuse surface.

Basin.history: Basin (usebasin.com) is a form backend service for static and Jamstack websites that handles form submissions, spam filtering, file uploads, and workflow automation without requiring server code. The service targets developers building static sites who need a managed form processing backend. It offers a freemium model with paid plans for higher submission volumes.
Basin.summary_abuse: Basin is a form backend service that processes HTML form submissions. Form backends can be used to facilitate spam submissions or scraping, but Basin includes spam filtering powered by SpamCheck.ai to mitigate this. No notable abuse incidents specific to Basin have been documented.

Basis Technologies.history: Basis Technologies was formerly known as Centro, a digital advertising software and services company founded in 2001 in Chicago, Illinois. The company rebranded to Basis Technologies in 2022 to better reflect its technology-first positioning. Basis offers a programmatic demand-side platform (DSP) and media automation suite used by advertising agencies and media buyers. The Basis DSP has held the G2 top DSP ranking every quarter since 2017.
Basis Technologies.summary_abuse: Basis Technologies is a programmatic advertising DSP, a category with inherent risk of ad fraud, brand safety issues, and malvertising. Legitimate DSPs like Basis implement anti-fraud measures and brand safety controls, but programmatic ad supply chains are complex and fraud in the broader ecosystem is persistent. No specific abuse incidents involving Basis Technologies have been widely publicized.

Bask Health.history: Bask Health is a telehealth infrastructure platform founded by Zachary Dorf. The company provides a no-code or API-driven platform for launching and operating direct-to-consumer digital healthcare brands, powering over 100 telehealth companies. The platform handles compliance, clinical workflows, and pharmacy integrations for telehealth startups.
Bask Health.summary_abuse: Bask Health operates in the telehealth infrastructure space, which carries regulatory risk related to controlled substance prescribing and patient data protection. Telehealth platforms have faced scrutiny over prescription practices for controlled medications. HIPAA compliance is critical given the health data handled. No specific public abuse incidents have been documented for Bask Health.

basket.js.history: basket.js was a small JavaScript library for script loading and caching in localStorage, created around 2012. It is no longer maintained and has been largely superseded by modern browser caching and bundlers.
basket.js.summary_abuse: basket.js is a legacy JavaScript script-loading library with no notable abuse signal. It has no server-side surface.

Batch.history: Batch is a French mobile CRM and push notification platform founded around 2014 and headquartered in Paris. The platform provides iOS and Android push notifications, in-app messaging, and marketing automation for mobile apps. The company reports an average 600% ROI for push notification campaigns and serves brands seeking mobile user retention.
Batch.summary_abuse: Batch is a mobile push notification platform with standard spam risk associated with mass notification services. Push notification platforms can be misused to send unwanted or deceptive messages to users. Batch provides marketing tools to legitimate mobile app developers and has no documented major abuse incidents. GDPR compliance is relevant given the French headquarters.

Batchbook.history: Batchbook was co-founded in 2006 by Pamela O'Hara in Providence, Rhode Island. The company launched at DEMO Fall 2007 and was bootstrapped from the founders' backyard. Batchbook provided a social CRM product designed for small businesses who needed customer relationship management without the complexity of enterprise solutions like Salesforce. The company has seen declining activity in recent years.
Batchbook.summary_abuse: Batchbook is a small business CRM tool with no documented abuse history. The platform handles contact and customer relationship data for small businesses. Its limited scale and B2B focus make it a low-risk product from an abuse perspective.

Batflat.history: Batflat is a lightweight PHP flat-file CMS first released in May 2016, created by a Polish developer. It was designed as a simple alternative to database-driven CMS platforms. The system stores all data in flat files, requires no MySQL installation, and supports multilingual sites. The project has been officially deprecated and the developers recommend using newer CMS alternatives.
Batflat.summary_abuse: Batflat is a self-hosted flat-file CMS that is now abandoned. As a self-hosted CMS, it presents no hosted service abuse surface. Its abandonment means unpatched security vulnerabilities could be exploited if deployed on publicly accessible servers.

Batman.js.history: Batman.js was created by Shopify and open-sourced around 2011. It was a CoffeeScript-based MVC framework for single-page web applications, inspired by Ruby on Rails conventions. Shopify used it to rebuild the merchant admin interface. The project was abandoned around 2014 when Shopify decided to move away from it, and the repository has been inactive since.
Batman.js.summary_abuse: Batman.js is an abandoned client-side JavaScript framework with no hosted service. It presents no meaningful abuse surface.

Baya.history: Baya is a simple mobile-first website builder targeting small businesses such as restaurants, cafes, barbers, and coaches. The platform allows users to build websites directly from their phone without coding. It offers an automatic layout system that handles design, hosting, image resizing, and basic SEO.
Baya.summary_abuse: Baya is a small website builder platform. Like any website builder that allows rapid site creation, it could be used to create spam or fraudulent small business websites. No documented abuse incidents specific to Baya have been identified.

Bazaarvoice Curation.history: Bazaarvoice was founded in 2005 in Austin, Texas. The company pioneered the ratings and reviews market for e-commerce, enabling brands and retailers to collect and display authentic user-generated content. It went public in 2012 on Nasdaq and was later taken private by Synacor in 2018. Bazaarvoice serves over 11,500 brands through a network of 13,000+ retailers for review syndication.
Bazaarvoice Curation.summary_abuse: Bazaarvoice handles user-generated content including product reviews and ratings at scale. The platform has implemented measures against fake reviews and review fraud. As a UGC platform, it faces ongoing challenges with review manipulation by brands seeking to inflate ratings. Bazaarvoice has been involved in regulatory scrutiny related to review authenticity. Their Curation product specifically filters UGC to surface relevant content.

Bazaarvoice Reviews.history: Bazaarvoice was founded in 2005 in Austin, Texas. The company pioneered the ratings and reviews market for e-commerce, enabling brands and retailers to collect and display authentic user-generated content. It went public in 2012 on Nasdaq and was later taken private by Synacor in 2018. Bazaarvoice serves over 11,500 brands through a network of 13,000+ retailers for review syndication.
Bazaarvoice Reviews.summary_abuse: Bazaarvoice handles user-generated content including product reviews and ratings at scale. The platform has implemented measures against fake reviews and review fraud. As a UGC platform, it faces ongoing challenges with review manipulation by brands seeking to inflate ratings. Bazaarvoice has been involved in regulatory scrutiny related to review authenticity. Their Curation product specifically filters UGC to surface relevant content.

Bazo.history: Bazo is a B2B lead generation and website personalization platform that identifies company visitors to websites and enables targeted popup and call-to-action campaigns. The tool provides lead source tracking, visitor segmentation, and scheduled alerts for sales teams. It is a niche SaaS product targeting B2B sales and marketing teams.
Bazo.summary_abuse: Bazo is a lead generation tool that tracks website visitors by attempting to identify the companies behind IP addresses. This type of behavioral tracking and data collection raises privacy concerns, as it involves building profiles of website visitors without explicit consent. The platform also enables popup and lead capture campaigns. No specific documented abuse incidents, but the tracking methodology is of the type flagged by GDPR enforcement.

bbPress.history: bbPress was started by Matt Mullenweg in 2004 as standalone forum software to power the WordPress.org support forums. It was later rebuilt as a WordPress plugin and has been stewarded by Automattic ever since. No notable forks or ownership changes.
bbPress.summary_abuse: bbPress is a WordPress forum plugin with no notable targeted abuse signal. User-generated content on forums can attract spam, but this is a generic risk managed by site administrators. There is no documented pattern of bbPress being specifically targeted or favoured for fraud.

bdok.history: bdok (bdok.ir) is an Iranian cloud-based platform enabling users to create and manage online stores without technical knowledge, primarily serving the Iranian e-commerce market.
bdok.summary_abuse: bdok is an Iranian no-code e-commerce store builder with a free tier. As a hosted store platform it carries some risk for scam storefronts, though no specific documented incidents are known.

BDOW.history: BDOW (formerly Sumo or SumoMe) is a suite of website growth and marketing tools including pop-ups, email capture, and social sharing. Founded around 2014. The product has been rebranded multiple times.
BDOW.summary_abuse: BDOW is a marketing popup and email capture tool. Popup tools of this type are sometimes used on scam or clickbait sites, but BDOW is a mainstream tool with no documented pattern of systematic abuse by bad actors.

Beacons.history: Beacons was founded in 2020 by Neal Jean, Jesse Zhang, Greg Luppescu, and David Zeng, all Stanford University PhD alumni. The company raised $6 million in seed funding in 2021. Beacons started as a link-in-bio tool for creators and expanded into a full creator monetization platform.
Beacons.summary_abuse: Beacons is a link-in-bio and creator monetization platform, a category that has been exploited for phishing and scam pages hosted under credible-looking creator profile URLs. Link-in-bio platforms with free tiers and custom domains are routinely targeted by threat actors creating fake brand and celebrity profiles. Beacons should be evaluated against its abuse reporting and takedown procedures, which are not publicly detailed.

Beae.history: Beae is a Shopify page builder app created by a Vietnamese development team. The app earned Shopify's Built for Shopify badge, indicating it meets Shopify's highest standards for performance, design, and reliability. It focuses on building high-converting landing pages and product pages for Shopify merchants without coding.
Beae.summary_abuse: Beae is a Shopify page builder used by legitimate merchants to create product and landing pages. The tool operates within the Shopify ecosystem and has no notable abuse history.

Beam AfterSell.history: AfterSell by BEAM Commerce is a Shopify app for post-purchase upsells and cross-sells, enabling merchants to present one-click offers after checkout without requiring customers to re-enter payment details. The app has a 4.7 star rating on the Shopify App Store and targets Shopify and Shopify Plus merchants seeking to increase average order value.
Beam AfterSell.summary_abuse: Beam AfterSell operates within the Shopify ecosystem and helps merchants present upsell offers after a completed purchase. The dark pattern risk is moderate, as post-purchase upsells are a known sales tactic that can feel manipulative to consumers. No documented fraud or malicious use cases have been reported.

Beam Impact.history: Beam Impact was founded to enable e-commerce brands to donate a percentage of purchases to nonprofits chosen by customers. The company raised $13.3 million in 2022 and serves over 130 mission-driven brands including Instacart and Roots Canada. The platform allows customers to direct charitable donations without additional cost.
Beam Impact.summary_abuse: Beam Impact facilitates charitable giving through e-commerce checkouts. The platform vets nonprofits in its network and the donation mechanism operates transparently. No notable abuse or fraud incidents have been documented.

Beam OutSell.history: Beam OutSell (OutSell) is a Shopify app by BEAM Commerce that provides frequently-bought-together product recommendations, discounted upsells, and cross-sell features for Shopify merchants. It is part of BEAM Commerce's suite of Shopify conversion optimization apps.
Beam OutSell.summary_abuse: Beam OutSell is a Shopify upsell app with no notable abuse history. It operates within the Shopify ecosystem and presents product recommendations to shoppers. The dark pattern risk is moderate given the upsell and cross-sell nature of the product.

Beamer.history: Beamer is a no-code product changelog and update notification SaaS tool. It enables SaaS product teams to announce new features, releases, and updates to users via an in-app widget and notification center. The platform has grown as a standalone tool in the product communication category.
Beamer.summary_abuse: Beamer is a product changelog widget embedded into SaaS applications. It has no notable abuse history and its functionality is limited to in-product notification display. The platform does not host user-generated content in a publicly accessible format.

Beamery.history: Beamery was founded in 2013 in London by Abakar Saidov, Sultan Saidov, and Mike Paterson. The company provides an AI-powered talent operating system for enterprise workforce management including recruiting, talent development, and workforce planning. Beamery reached unicorn status in December 2022 after raising $50 million in its Series D round.
Beamery.summary_abuse: Beamery is an enterprise HR and talent management platform handling sensitive employee and candidate personal data. As an enterprise HR system, it is subject to GDPR and other data protection regulations. No notable public abuse or fraud incidents have been documented.

Beans.history: Beans is a customer loyalty and rewards platform for e-commerce, available as both a Shopify app and a WooCommerce plugin. The platform enables merchants to run points-based loyalty programs, referral programs, and customer retention campaigns. It integrates with email marketing tools like Klaviyo and Omnisend.
Beans.summary_abuse: Beans is a loyalty rewards plugin for e-commerce with no notable abuse history. Loyalty programs can be gamed with fake referrals or fraudulent point collection, which is a standard challenge for the category. The platform is a legitimate tool for merchant retention programs.

Bear Blog.history: Bear Blog was created by Herman Martinus as a privacy-first, minimal blogging platform with no JavaScript, no trackers, and no stylesheets. The project was originally open-source under the MIT license but transitioned to a source-available Elastic license in 2025 after repeated instances of the code being forked into competing hosting services with minimal changes. The platform operates as a freemium SaaS model.
Bear Blog.summary_abuse: Bear Blog is a minimalist blogging platform with no trackers or JavaScript. The free tier could theoretically be used to host spam blogs, but the minimal nature of the platform and the operator's involvement in reviewing content limits this risk. The source-available license change in 2025 addressed a different concern about code reuse rather than abuse.

Beaver Builder.history: Beaver Builder was created in 2014 by three founders who ran a web design agency in Campbell, California. They built it to solve the need for a professional-grade WordPress page builder that non-technical clients could edit. The plugin grew to trust from over one million websites and a team of 20+ people.
Beaver Builder.summary_abuse: Beaver Builder is a WordPress page builder used by legitimate developers and agencies. Like any widely-used WordPress plugin, nulled versions circulate on piracy sites and may contain malware. No incidents of the official plugin being used maliciously have been documented.

BedBooking.history: BedBooking originated in 2012 as an internal project at RST Software Masters, a Polish software company. The product was built to develop mobile platform competencies and grew into a standalone vacation rental and hotel booking management system. BedBooking has been adopted by over 140,000 property managers for reservation calendar and channel management.
BedBooking.summary_abuse: BedBooking is a property management and booking system for hotels and vacation rentals. It is a legitimate B2B tool with no notable abuse history. Guest data including personal and payment information is processed, so data security practices are important considerations.

Beddy.history: Beddy is a hospitality management software platform designed to streamline hotel operations, enhance guest experiences, and improve efficiency in hospitality establishments. The platform provides property management system features for hotels and other accommodation providers.
Beddy.summary_abuse: Beddy is a niche hospitality management software with no notable abuse history. As a B2B hotel management system, it handles guest data and reservation information in a regulated context.

Beehiiv.history: Beehiiv was founded in 2021 by Tyler Denk, Benjamin Hargett, and Jake Hurd, who previously worked on the technology infrastructure at Morning Brew. The platform provides newsletter publishing and monetization tools for independent creators and media companies. The company raised $33 million in a Series B round in April 2024, bringing total funding to approximately $50 million. The platform has sent over 35 billion emails for more than 55,000 creators.
Beehiiv.summary_abuse: Beehiiv is a newsletter publishing platform with a free tier that could be used to send spam or phishing emails at scale. Email marketing platforms are a known vector for spam campaigns when misused. Beehiiv has grown rapidly and serves many legitimate publishers, but its email delivery infrastructure could be exploited. The platform should have anti-spam and AUP enforcement in place.

Beehiiv RSS feed.history: The Beehiiv RSS feed is an automatically generated RSS feed produced by the Beehiiv newsletter platform for each publisher's blog or newsletter archive. It enables RSS readers and aggregators to subscribe to Beehiiv-hosted newsletter content. Beehiiv was founded in 2021 by former Morning Brew employees.
Beehiiv RSS feed.summary_abuse: Beehiiv RSS feeds are read-only syndication endpoints for newsletter content published on the Beehiiv platform. RSS feeds carry minimal abuse risk as they are content syndication endpoints rather than interactive services.

Beeketing.history: Beeketing was founded in 2011 with offices in Vietnam and the United States. The company provides a marketing automation suite for WooCommerce and other e-commerce platforms, offering upsell, cross-sell, behavioral email, and conversion optimization tools. The platform claims to have generated nearly $1.7 billion in additional sales for merchants worldwide.
Beeketing.summary_abuse: Beeketing provides e-commerce marketing automation including aggressive upsell and exit popup features that sit in the dark pattern risk category. The platform embeds tracking scripts on merchant websites to profile visitor behavior. No documented fraud or malware incidents, but the aggressive conversion optimization tactics raise dark pattern concerns.

Beeshop.history: Beeshop is an e-commerce platform operated by Sky Victory Management Ltd., a Hong Kong-based company. The platform launched an E-Commerce 5.0 model in 2025 focused on O2O commerce across Southeast Asia. The company has deep roots in China's supply chain and logistics ecosystem.
Beeshop.summary_abuse: Beeshop is a relatively unknown e-commerce marketplace associated with a Hong Kong company and primarily targeting Southeast Asian markets. The limited public information about the company's practices, ownership, and security posture warrants caution. The platform's reward-based commerce model and unclear AUP enforcement suggest elevated potential for abuse through counterfeit goods or payment fraud.

Beeswax.history: Beeswax was founded in 2014 in New York by former Google and DoubleClick advertising executives. The company pioneered the Bidder-as-a-Service (BaaS) model, enabling sophisticated media buyers to build custom programmatic buying strategies. FreeWheel, a Comcast company, acquired Beeswax and integrated it into its programmatic advertising stack.
Beeswax.summary_abuse: Beeswax is a programmatic advertising DSP and bidder-as-a-service platform. Programmatic advertising is a category associated with ad fraud, brand safety risks, and malvertising through complex supply chains. As a sophisticated B2B tool used by experienced media buyers, direct abuse is less common than with self-serve platforms. Comcast/FreeWheel ownership provides institutional accountability.

Beezer.history: Beezer is a no-code progressive web app builder based in the UK that enables small businesses to create PWAs without coding. The company appeared on BBC's Dragons' Den in August 2018 to pitch its concept of making mobile app creation accessible. The platform offers a drag-and-drop interface with templates for various business types.
Beezer.summary_abuse: Beezer is a no-code PWA builder with no notable abuse history. Like all website and app builders, it could be used to create deceptive applications, but no documented cases exist.

Behzi.history: Behzi is an Iranian all-in-one platform providing services including event organization, tour management, school registrations, and store discounts. The platform serves the Iranian domestic market and operates primarily in Farsi.
Behzi.summary_abuse: Behzi is an Iranian consumer services platform. Given the jurisdictional context and the nature of multi-service platforms in restricted markets, users outside Iran should note limited regulatory oversight compared to Western standards. No specific abuse incidents have been documented.

Belliza.history: Belliza is a premium Shopify theme created by TemplatesCoder, designed for modern brands building online storefronts. The theme focuses on performance and design for e-commerce merchants on the Shopify platform.
Belliza.summary_abuse: Belliza is a Shopify theme with no notable abuse history. As a theme product, it could be used to create fraudulent storefronts, but this is a risk common to all commercial themes rather than specific to Belliza.

BEM.history: BEM (Block Element Modifier) is a CSS naming methodology and architecture standard developed by the Yandex engineering team. It originated around 2005-2006 internally at Yandex and was formally documented and open-sourced starting in 2009. In 2010, Yandex created a bem organization on GitHub to share the methodology publicly. BEM has since become one of the most widely adopted CSS naming conventions in the web development community, used independently of any Yandex tools. No ownership changes or controversies.
BEM.summary_abuse: BEM is a CSS naming methodology with no abuse profile. It is a frontend code architecture standard, not software or a service, and cannot be used for malicious purposes. There is no vendor, no hosted infrastructure, and no security surface.

Bench.history: Bench Accounting was co-founded in 2012 by Ian Crosby in Vancouver, Canada. The company built a hybrid bookkeeping model combining proprietary software with in-house bookkeepers for small business clients. In late 2024, Bench faced financial difficulties, defaulted on over $65 million in debt, and filed for bankruptcy in Canada on January 7, 2025. The company was acquired by Employer.com and resumed operations in January 2025.
Bench.summary_abuse: Bench Accounting is a legitimate bookkeeping service for small businesses. The company's 2024 bankruptcy and abrupt service disruption caused significant harm to thousands of small business customers who lost access to financial records and services during tax season. The company was acquired and resumed operations. No fraud or malicious abuse incidents beyond the business insolvency event.

Benchmark.history: Benchmark Email was founded in 2004 and is headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. The company has been a long-standing email marketing platform for small to medium businesses, offering drag-and-drop email building, templates, and automation. In October 2025, Benchmark Email relaunched its platform with a modern architecture and brand identity refresh.
Benchmark.summary_abuse: Benchmark Email is a bulk email marketing platform. Email marketing platforms with free tiers are routinely used to send spam, phishing, and scam campaigns. Benchmark has been listed in various spam complaints over the years, which is common for email service providers. The platform publishes an AUP and spam policy, but the efficacy of enforcement varies. Users should evaluate deliverability and compliance practices.

Bento.history: Bento is a marketing automation and CRM platform targeting small businesses and indie founders. The platform provides email marketing, automation, and behavioral analytics in a developer-friendly package. It competes against larger email marketing platforms by focusing on power users and technical founders.
Bento.summary_abuse: Bento is a marketing automation and email platform with standard email marketing abuse risk. The platform targets technical founders and small businesses. No notable abuse incidents have been documented.

Bentobox.history: BentoBox was co-founded in 2013 by Krystle Mobayeni in New York City as a website and marketing platform specifically for restaurants. The platform grew to serve over 4,000 restaurants across 50 states. BentoBox was acquired by Fiserv and rebranded as Clover, integrating its restaurant marketing capabilities into the Clover commerce platform.
Bentobox.summary_abuse: BentoBox is a restaurant website and marketing platform now operating under the Clover brand by Fiserv. As a platform for legitimate restaurant businesses, it has no notable abuse history. Payment data is processed through Fiserv's PCI-compliant infrastructure.

Bernet Cloud.history: Bernet Cloud is a cloud management platform that enables global application deployment without requiring advanced technical expertise. Limited public information is available about the company's history or backing.
Bernet Cloud.summary_abuse: Bernet Cloud is a small cloud management platform with limited public documentation. Its obscurity and lack of verifiable security practices or regulatory compliance make it a higher-risk option compared to established cloud providers. No specific abuse incidents have been documented.

BerqWP.history: BerqWP was created by Hamza Mairaj of Berqier Ltd, a web designer and WordPress developer who built the plugin to automate the process of achieving Core Web Vitals scores above 90 for WordPress sites. The plugin became available on the WordPress.org repository and offers both a free local optimization mode and a premium cloud-powered optimization service.
BerqWP.summary_abuse: BerqWP is a WordPress performance optimization plugin with no notable abuse history. As a WordPress plugin, security of the code and the integrity of the update channel are key considerations. The plugin is available on the official WordPress.org repository.

BespokeChat.history: BespokeChat is a Polish-developed real-time online chat solution for websites. The platform provides live chat functionality for customer support and sales. Limited public information is available about the company's history and funding.
BespokeChat.summary_abuse: BespokeChat is a live chat widget for websites. Live chat tools are occasionally used for social engineering attacks on website visitors, but there are no documented cases of BespokeChat being specifically associated with abuse.

Bestie.history: Bestie is an AI-powered post-purchase survey platform for Shopify brands. The platform automates survey creation and analysis to help merchants understand customer feedback and attribution. It is a recently launched Shopify app targeting D2C brands.
Bestie.summary_abuse: Bestie is an AI-powered survey tool for Shopify merchants with no notable abuse history. The tool collects customer survey data which is managed by the merchant. No documented abuse cases.

BeTheme.history: BeTheme is a premium multipurpose WordPress theme created by Muffin Group, first released on ThemeForest around 2013. It became one of the best-selling WordPress themes with over 700 pre-built website templates. The theme is sold exclusively through Envato's ThemeForest marketplace for a one-time fee of $59, including lifetime updates.
BeTheme.summary_abuse: BeTheme is a WordPress theme product used to build websites. Like any popular website template, it can be used to create legitimate sites or fraudulent fake websites. There are no documented incidents of the theme itself being weaponized, but fake clones and nulled versions of premium themes are distributed on piracy sites, which may contain malicious code.

Better Cart.history: Better Cart is an AI-powered e-commerce checkout optimization platform that uses predictive machine learning models to improve checkout conversion rates. The product targets online retailers seeking to reduce cart abandonment.
Better Cart.summary_abuse: Better Cart is a checkout optimization tool for e-commerce merchants. No notable abuse history has been documented.

Better Click To Tweet.history: Better Click To Tweet is a free WordPress plugin that adds stylized click-to-tweet boxes to blog posts, allowing readers to share highlighted quotes on Twitter. It is a simple utility plugin available in the WordPress.org plugin repository.
Better Click To Tweet.summary_abuse: Better Click To Tweet is a simple WordPress plugin that adds tweet buttons to blog posts. It has no server-side component, no data collection, and no abuse surface.

Better Price.history: Better Price is a Shopify app by Architechpro that enables merchants to offer discount coupons to verified leads who request lower pricing. The app is designed to gate discounts to genuine purchase intent rather than offering blanket promotional codes.
Better Price.summary_abuse: Better Price is a Shopify discount management app. The app's mechanism of providing coupons on price request could be gamed by coupon-harvesting bots, which is a risk common to e-commerce discount tools. No documented specific abuse incidents.

Better Replay.history: Better Replay is a session recording and replay platform that captures user interactions on websites for UX analysis. The platform targets web developers and marketers seeking to understand how users navigate their sites.
Better Replay.summary_abuse: Better Replay is a session recording tool that captures user interactions on websites. Session replay tools collect sensitive user behavior data including form inputs in some configurations. The platform should implement PII masking, but users of sites running such tools may not be aware of the recording. No documented specific abuse incidents.

Better Search.history: Better Search is a WordPress plugin created by Ajay D'Souza (WebberZone) around 2009 to replace the default WordPress search with relevance-based results using MySQL full-text indexing. It has been actively maintained for over a decade with a Pro version added later for advanced features such as search highlighting and analytics.
Better Search.summary_abuse: No known abuse, malware, or privacy concerns. It is an open-source GPL plugin widely available on WordPress.org with a clean history and a reputable independent developer behind it.

Better Stack.history: Better Stack was founded in 2021 in Prague, Czech Republic by Veronika Kolejak and Juraj Masar. Originally launched as Better Uptime (an uptime monitoring tool), the company rebranded to Better Stack in 2023 to reflect its expanded observability platform covering logs, uptime, and incident management. It raised $18.6M in a Series A led by Creandum in July 2022 and a further $10M from KAYA VC in January 2024, totaling $28.6M. By 2024 it had over 200,000 developers and 4,000 paying customers and reached profitability in 2023.
Better Stack.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents or security breaches. Better Stack is a well-regarded developer tooling company with transparent pricing and a strong reputation in the DevOps community. No dark patterns or privacy red flags identified.

BetterBot.history: BetterBot was founded in 2018 by Robert Turnbull and Zlatko Bogoevski and is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia. The platform offers conversational AI leasing solutions for the multifamily real estate market, automating lead qualification, tour scheduling, and resident communication. It serves property management companies across the US.
BetterBot.summary_abuse: No notable abuse, data breach, or regulatory violations have been reported for BetterBot. The platform collects prospective tenant contact information and leasing data, which carries inherent privacy considerations, but no specific incidents are documented.

BetterDocs.history: BetterDocs is a knowledge base and documentation plugin created by WPDeveloper, a Bangladesh-based WordPress product company, launched in 2019. It grew rapidly, reaching 10,000 users within its first year and over 40,000 active installs by the mid-2020s. It offers both a WordPress plugin and a standalone documentation hosting solution.
BetterDocs.summary_abuse: No known abuse or security incidents. BetterDocs is developed by WPDeveloper, a trusted WordPress plugin studio with millions of users across multiple products. No privacy concerns or dark patterns have been reported.

BetterDocs plugin.history: BetterDocs plugin is the WordPress-specific plugin edition of the BetterDocs knowledge base platform, developed by WPDeveloper (Bangladesh) and first launched in 2019. It surpassed 10,000 active installs within its first year and has grown to over 40,000 active installs on WordPress.org. The Pro version adds features such as live search, analytics, and multiple knowledge base support.
BetterDocs plugin.summary_abuse: No known abuse, malware, or security incidents. The plugin is GPL-licensed, hosted on WordPress.org, and developed by WPDeveloper, a well-known WordPress plugin studio. No privacy or dark pattern concerns have been reported.

Bettermode.history: Bettermode (formerly Tribe Technologies) was founded in 2018 by Siavash Mahmoudian, Soheil Alavi, and Mohsen Malayeri in Toronto, Ontario, Canada. Initially launched as Tribe, a white-label community platform, the company rebranded to Bettermode in 2022 and repositioned toward mid-market and enterprise customers. The free plan was discontinued in March 2026 with pricing starting at $49/month.
Bettermode.summary_abuse: No known abuse incidents or security breaches. Bettermode is a legitimate community platform with public pricing and standard SaaS practices. No documented dark patterns or privacy violations beyond typical SaaS data collection.

Betty Blocks.history: Founded in the Netherlands circa 2012 by Chris and Tim Obdam. Betty Blocks is a no-code platform for building enterprise business applications. The company raised EUR 27.97M to expand its citizen development platform.
Betty Blocks.summary_abuse: Betty Blocks is an enterprise no-code application development platform. It has no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B platform used by businesses to build internal applications and portals, with no consumer-facing features typically exploited for fraud.

Beusable.history: Beusable is a UX analytics and heatmap platform developed by 4Grit, a South Korean startup founded around 2015 in Pangyo, Gyeonggi-do, South Korea. The product launched in open beta in 2017 and gained traction with enterprise clients including Samsung Electronics, LG Electronics, and Dentsu. Beusable focuses on quantitative UX analysis with session recording and heatmap visualization for web and mobile properties.
Beusable.summary_abuse: Beusable is a UX analytics tool that embeds tracking scripts on client websites to collect visitor behavior data. As a behavior analytics platform, it collects detailed user interaction data including mouse movements, clicks, and scroll patterns, which raises standard privacy considerations for end users of sites using it. No documented abuse cases involving misuse of the platform for fraud or phishing have been reported.

Bevy.history: Bevy was founded in 2017 by Derek Andersen, Joel Fernandes, and Alex Bendig in Palo Alto, California, growing out of the Startup Grind conference network. The company raised a $61.4M Series C in March 2021 with participation from Accel and a broad set of community-focused investors. The platform targets enterprise companies that run distributed user-community and developer-advocacy programs.
Bevy.summary_abuse: Bevy is an enterprise community and virtual events platform used by large organizations to run developer or user groups. It has no notable abuse signal; the platform requires enterprise contracts and is not consumer-accessible, which eliminates the primary vectors for phishing, scam storefronts, or spam that consumer-facing platforms face. No documented abuse incidents have been identified.

Beyable.history: Beyable was founded in 2014 in Paris, France by Julien Dugaret and Florian Papillon as a personalization and conversion rate optimization SaaS targeting e-commerce brands. The company raised seed funding from Paris Business Angels and grew to 34 employees. On January 13, 2026, Beyable was acquired by SaleCycle, a behavioral marketing platform.
Beyable.summary_abuse: Beyable has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B conversion optimization and personalization platform used by online retailers to tailor website experiences to visitor behavior. It embeds JavaScript on client websites to collect behavioral data, which carries standard tracking considerations but is not associated with fraudulent use. No documented abuse incidents have been identified.

Beyond.history: Beyond (getbeyond.io) is a small US-based SaaS product offering slide-out panels and content overlay widgets for websites. Founded around 2017 in Princeton, NJ area. The company is a micro-startup with very limited public profile and no known funding or acquisition history.
Beyond.summary_abuse: Beyond is a niche website widget tool providing slide-out content panels. It has no notable abuse signal and no consumer-facing features that would attract fraud activity. The tool is installed on merchant sites and has minimal attack surface for abuse.

BeyondMenu.history: BeyondMenu was founded in 2009 in Atlanta, Georgia as an online food ordering platform for independent restaurants. It partners with over 15,000 local restaurants across 2,000+ US cities. The company received investment from Bregal Sagemount and was named to Inc.'s 2023 Fastest-Growing Private Companies list.
BeyondMenu.summary_abuse: BeyondMenu is an online food ordering platform for independent restaurants with no notable abuse signal specific to its tech. Like any restaurant ordering platform, individual merchants could potentially list fake businesses, but the platform serves verified local restaurant partners and there are no documented systemic fraud issues.

Beyonk.history: Founded in 2019 by Oscar White and Antony Jones in the UK. Raised $10M in venture funding. Acquired BookingHound in 2022 to expand into activity centres and tour operator segments.
Beyonk.summary_abuse: Beyonk is a ticketing and booking SaaS for visitor attractions with no notable abuse signal. It processes payments through Stripe, does not store card details, and serves legitimate event and attraction businesses. The freemium model allows free use by passing booking fees to customers, but this is standard industry practice rather than an abuse vector. No public bug bounty or responsible disclosure policy found. The platform does not offer anonymous signup or custom-domain hosting, limiting its attractiveness to bad actors.

Biano.history: Biano was founded in 2015 in Prague, Czech Republic, by Michael Zelinka, Michal Tvrznik, and Arno Mulder. Czech investment group Miton invested at founding and holds a majority stake (~87%). The company operates a furniture and home decor comparison marketplace across multiple European and South American markets, and offers the Biano Pixel as a remarketing analytics tool for e-shops listing products on its platform.
Biano.summary_abuse: Biano Pixel is a niche remarketing/analytics tracking pixel used exclusively by furniture e-shops participating in the Biano marketplace. Its scope is narrow and limited to partner retailers, with no meaningful abuse signal documented. As a tracking pixel, it collects behavioral data on user interactions with furniture listings, which carries inherent privacy considerations under GDPR, but there are no known reports of it being exploited for phishing, fraud, or scam infrastructure. Abuse risk is low given its closed ecosystem and B2B-only distribution.

Bidmatic.history: Bidmatic was founded in 2021 as a publisher-centric ad monetization platform offering header bidding, SSP, and yield optimization tools. No acquisitions or notable ownership changes have been reported.
Bidmatic.summary_abuse: Bidmatic is an ad-tech platform that by design embeds third-party demand partners and real-time bidding infrastructure across publisher websites, resulting in heavy tracker density on any page using it. Like other SSP and header bidding platforms, it can serve as a vector for malvertising if demand-side partners are not carefully vetted — a documented risk category for programmatic advertising intermediaries. Bidmatic publishes a privacy policy and DPA oriented toward GDPR compliance, signaling some anti-abuse posture. No specific public incidents of abuse or malvertising campaigns have been attributed to Bidmatic directly, but the platform operates in an ad-tech ecosystem where such risks are structural.

Big Cartel.history: Big Cartel was founded in 2005 in Salt Lake City, Utah by Eric Turner and Matt Wigham as an ecommerce platform designed specifically for independent artists, musicians, and makers. The company has remained bootstrapped and independent with no outside investment, maintaining a strong identity around supporting creative entrepreneurs.
Big Cartel.summary_abuse: Big Cartel is an ecommerce platform targeting artists and makers with a free tier for up to 5 products. Its low barrier to entry and free hosted storefronts have occasionally been used for fraudulent product listings or counterfeit goods, consistent with any open ecommerce platform, but no systemic or large-scale abuse patterns have been publicly documented.

BIGACE.history: BIGACE is a free open-source PHP/MySQL CMS that was active in the mid-2000s. The project is now effectively abandoned with no recent activity on GitHub.
BIGACE.summary_abuse: BIGACE has no notable abuse signal. It is an abandoned open-source CMS with no hosted service surface.

BigCommerce.history: BigCommerce was founded in 2009 in Sydney, Australia by Mitchell Harper and Eddie Machaalani, later relocating its headquarters to Austin, Texas. It went public on NASDAQ (BIGC) in August 2020. It competes directly with Shopify in the enterprise e-commerce segment and has remained independent. No notable acquisitions or deprecations.
BigCommerce.summary_abuse: BigCommerce hosts thousands of online stores and, like all hosted e-commerce platforms, is occasionally used to set up fraudulent or counterfeit storefronts. The company publishes an Acceptable Use Policy and has a trust and safety team that responds to merchant fraud reports. As a publicly traded company with PCI DSS Level 1 certification, it maintains compliance and fraud-detection infrastructure. Abuse is a small share of overall use and the vendor responds to takedown requests.

BigCommerce B2B Edition.history: BigCommerce B2B Edition was launched in June 2021 as BundleB2B, a third-party app providing advanced B2B ecommerce features for BigCommerce merchants. BigCommerce acquired BundleB2B in April 2022 and integrated it as an official product called B2B Edition, making it the native B2B solution for the BigCommerce platform.
BigCommerce B2B Edition.summary_abuse: BigCommerce B2B Edition has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B ecommerce feature set for wholesale and manufacturing businesses, operating within BigCommerce's vetted merchant ecosystem with no anonymous or free-tier access.

BigDataCloud IP Geolocation.history: BigDataCloud was founded in 2018 in Adelaide, South Australia by Pol Nisenblat. The company focuses on providing highly accurate IP geolocation APIs backed by proprietary patent-protected technology (US Patent 11,792,110). It remains a small independent operation with 2-10 employees.
BigDataCloud IP Geolocation.summary_abuse: BigDataCloud is an IP geolocation and reverse geocoding API service. IP geolocation APIs can be abused by fraud detection bypass attempts or to geolocate targets, but this is a characteristic of the API category rather than specific misuse of BigDataCloud. The service is primarily used by developers for analytics and compliance, with a free tier requiring API key registration. No documented specific abuse incidents.

Bigin.history: Bigin was launched in 2020 by Zoho Corporation as a lightweight CRM product specifically targeting small businesses, positioned as a simpler and more affordable alternative to Zoho CRM. It operates as a product line within Zoho, a large private Indian software company founded in 1996.
Bigin.summary_abuse: Bigin has no notable abuse signal. It is a CRM SaaS tool used primarily by small businesses for contact and pipeline management. It does not offer arbitrary content hosting, custom-domain publishing, URL shortening, or mass-email features that would attract fraudsters. Zoho Corporation maintains a published AUP and compliance framework across its product suite.

BIGLIST.history: BIGLIST is a long-running email newsletter management service operating since the late 1990s. It is a legacy email list hosting system still serving some established newsletters.
BIGLIST.summary_abuse: BIGLIST is an email newsletter hosting service. Email list services carry inherent spam risk, though BIGLIST is an opt-in system by design and is a very niche legacy service with no documented large-scale abuse patterns.

BigMarker.history: BigMarker was founded in 2011 in Chicago, Illinois as a browser-based webinar and virtual events platform. It has grown to support thousands of companies and universities for live streaming, webinars, and hybrid events. The company remains privately held with no disclosed acquisitions.
BigMarker.summary_abuse: BigMarker is a webinar and virtual events platform. Like all livestreaming and online event platforms, it could theoretically be used to host fraudulent investment seminars or phishing-oriented virtual events, which is a general risk category for the sector. No specific documented abuse incidents are on record for BigMarker specifically.

BigPoint.history: Founded in 2002 by Heiko Hubertz in Hamburg, Germany as a browser game developer and portal. Received USD 110M investment from NBC Universal and GMT Communications in 2008, then USD 350M from TA Associates and Summit Partners in 2011. Acquired by Chinese publisher Youzu Interactive in 2016 for EUR 80 million. Notable titles include Drakensang Online, DarkOrbit, Seafight, and Farmerama.
BigPoint.summary_abuse: Bigpoint operates free-to-play browser-based MMO games with over 200 million registered users. As with most free-to-play online game platforms, it faces challenges from account farming, botting, and virtual currency fraud, which are common across the free-to-play gaming industry. The company operates under German GDPR compliance as a subsidiary of Youzu Interactive. No major public abuse incidents specific to Bigpoint were documented beyond typical free-to-play gaming abuse vectors.

Bigshop.history: Bigshop is a Brazilian ecommerce platform created by Big Bang Digital Ltda, founded by Thiago Alexandre Valle de Oliveira and Joao Gabriel Torres de Oliveira Alves Ferreira in Belo Horizonte, MG. The Big Bang Digital parent company was established in October 2014, and the Bigshop platform domain was registered in June 2019. There have been no notable acquisitions, ownership changes, forks, or deprecation events since launch.
Bigshop.summary_abuse: Bigshop is a paid-subscription ecommerce SaaS for the Brazilian market with no free tier (only a 15-day trial) and no anonymous signup, as Brazilian tax ID (CNPJ/CPF) is required. The paid barrier and regional focus limit its appeal to fraudsters compared to free-tier global ecommerce platforms. No notable abuse incidents, phishing campaigns, or scam storefront patterns have been publicly documented. The vendor is a small micro-enterprise with limited anti-abuse infrastructure, but the platform's characteristics make abuse unlikely at scale.

BigTree CMS.history: BigTree CMS was created internally at Fastspot, a Baltimore-based digital design agency, around 2008 to power their client websites, and open-sourced under the LGPL in 2011. The project has no notable ownership changes; it remains a community-maintained project backed by Fastspot. As of 2024, development has shifted to maintenance mode with bug fixes and PHP 8.x compatibility work, but no major new features are planned.
BigTree CMS.summary_abuse: BigTree CMS has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted, open-source CMS requiring server access to deploy, with no consumer-facing hosted service, no free-tier cloud hosting, and no URL shortening or file-hosting features that scammers typically exploit. Like any CMS it could be installed on a compromised or malicious server, but it is not specifically targeted or preferred by threat actors.

Bigware.history: Bigware was a German open-source PHP ecommerce platform, originally created by Dirk Pagador around 2004. Distributed under the GPL license, it was downloaded over 200,000 times and developed by Bigware LTD. The project became dormant after ~2014, with the founder moving on to develop the AberLos Shopsoftware through Produktwunder Ltd.
Bigware.summary_abuse: Bigware is a dormant open-source PHP ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It is self-hosted shop software, not a hosted platform, and does not provide any consumer-facing services, free tier with custom domains, messaging, or file-sharing features. No documented history of being specifically targeted for phishing, scams, or fraud beyond the general risk of any outdated self-hosted ecommerce software.

Bikayi.history: Bikayi was founded on April 1, 2019 in Hyderabad, India by Sonakshi Nathani and Ashutosh Singla, two IIIT Hyderabad graduates. The platform helps small and medium Indian businesses build WhatsApp-integrated online stores.
