It raised $12.8 million from Y Combinator, Sequoia Capital India (Peak XV Partners), and Pioneer Fund, accumulating over 4 million registered users.
Bikayi.summary_abuse: Bikayi is an Indian e-commerce platform for small businesses with WhatsApp integration. Its free tier and low-friction onboarding for small merchants carry some risk of fake or fraudulent storefronts targeting Indian consumers, consistent with other open commerce platforms in emerging markets. No specific large-scale abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

Bileto.history: Bileto was founded in 2013 in Prague, Czech Republic as a cloud-based ticket sales and inspection platform for public transport operators. The company serves small and medium-sized transport agencies primarily in Central and Eastern Europe. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are recorded.
Bileto.summary_abuse: Bileto is a B2B ticketing and inspection platform for public transport operators with no notable abuse signal. The platform is used by transport agencies for managing ticket sales and validation, with no consumer-facing features that would attract fraud or phishing activity.

Billbee.history: Billbee originated in 2009 as a private tool built by founder Jan Krause to generate invoices and address labels for his wife's DaWanda shop, then officially launched as a commercial SaaS product in 2015. In November 2023, private equity firm Bregal Unternehmerkapital took a majority stake in Billbee, which subsequently became the nucleus of the comrce software group, bringing together Billbee, Replyco, VentoryOne, and other e-commerce tools.
Billbee.summary_abuse: Billbee is an order management and inventory SaaS aimed at small e-commerce merchants in the DACH region, with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a backend operational tool with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters typically target, no file hosting or URL shortening features, and no anonymous signup. Abuse risk is minimal.

Billgang.history: Billgang launched its digital commerce platform in February 2024 in Austin, Texas. It targets internet entrepreneurs and creators selling digital products, offering features like storefront design, fraud protection, and payment routing. The company is very early stage with approximately 5 employees and no disclosed funding.
Billgang.summary_abuse: Billgang is a digital product commerce platform primarily targeting creators and internet entrepreneurs. Its free tier, anonymous-friendly positioning toward internet entrepreneurs, and digital goods focus create meaningful risk for sale of stolen accounts, cracked software, unauthorized digital goods, and other grey-market digital products. The platform category historically attracts such listings on similar platforms (e.g., Sellix, Selly). No compliance certifications and no disclosed AUP enforcement track record given its very recent launch in 2024.

BILLmanager.history: BILLmanager is a hosting billing and provisioning panel developed by ISPsystem, a Russian software company. It has been used by hosting providers globally for billing automation. No major ownership changes documented.
BILLmanager.summary_abuse: BILLmanager is a billing management panel for hosting providers. Russian-origin hosting software carries some risk consideration for data handling, and the platform being used by hosting providers means it indirectly enables hosting services. No specific documented abuse incidents with the software itself.

Binance Pay.history: Binance Pay is a cryptocurrency payment product operated by Binance, the world's largest crypto exchange by volume, founded in 2017 by Changpeng Zhao. In 2023, Binance and its CEO pleaded guilty to US federal criminal charges including money laundering violations and paid $4.3 billion in fines. Zhao stepped down as CEO. The exchange continues to operate under new leadership with increased regulatory oversight.
Binance Pay.summary_abuse: Binance Pay is a cryptocurrency payment technology operated by Binance, which has a documented history of regulatory violations. In 2023, Binance paid $4.3 billion in US fines for money laundering and sanctions violations. Cryptocurrency payment products in general are frequently used in fraud, scams, and money laundering due to pseudonymous transactions. Binance Pay specifically has been observed in crypto scam payment flows. The parent company's compliance posture has materially improved post-2023 settlement, but residual risk remains elevated versus regulated fiat payment processors.

BinderPOS.history: BinderPOS was founded in 2018 in New Zealand by Joshua Grant, Rhys Glaskin, and David Logan to serve local game stores with POS and inventory management built on Shopify. In July 2022, TCGplayer acquired BinderPOS to strengthen its ecosystem for trading card game retailers.
BinderPOS.summary_abuse: BinderPOS is a niche POS platform for game stores with no notable abuse signal. It is a backend retail tool with no consumer-facing content-hosting features that scammers typically exploit.

Birdeye.history: Birdeye was founded in 2012 and is headquartered in Palo Alto, California. The company raised $93M across three funding rounds, including a $60M Series C in 2022, backed by investors including Salesforce founder Marc Benioff and Yahoo co-founder Jerry Yang. It has grown to serve over 80,000 customers across industries including healthcare, real estate, and automotive.
Birdeye.summary_abuse: Birdeye has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B customer experience and reputation management platform sold to multi-location businesses, with no consumer-facing hosting surface or anonymous signup. Its marketing automation and review management features are oriented toward legitimate business use. The platform is enterprise-gated with identity-verified accounts, reducing risk of anonymous misuse. No documented abuse patterns or security-research incidents were found.

BirdSeed.history: BirdSeed is a small US-based SaaS offering a suite of website engagement tools including live chat, video chat, and CRM integrations. No notable ownership changes or acquisitions are on record.
BirdSeed.summary_abuse: BirdSeed is a small live-chat and engagement SaaS with no notable abuse signal. The platform targets small business websites and has no consumer-facing content hosting or anonymous signup features that would make it attractive for phishing or spam operations.

biskoui.history: biskoui is a Swiss consent management and cookie compliance platform. Limited public information is available about its founding year.
biskoui.summary_abuse: biskoui is a cookie and consent management SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is a compliance tool used by website operators and has no content-hosting features that fraudsters would exploit.

Bitcoin.history: Created in 2008 by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto, with the Bitcoin whitepaper published in October 2008 and the network launching in January 2009. Bitcoin introduced the blockchain concept and proof-of-work consensus mechanism. Development is coordinated through multiple open-source teams, with Bitcoin Core being the primary reference client. No central company, foundation, or ownership changes.
Bitcoin.summary_abuse: Bitcoin is notably used in ransomware payments, darknet marketplace transactions, and cryptocurrency scams including Ponzi schemes, fake exchanges, and wallet phishing. Its pseudonymous and irreversible transaction model makes it attractive for illicit payments. However, legitimate use as a store of value and payment method now dominates transaction volume. Blockchain analysis has significantly improved traceability. Since Bitcoin is decentralized with no central vendor, there is no formal anti-abuse takedown process, though exchanges and blockchain analytics firms actively monitor suspicious transactions.

BITE.history: BITE GmbH was founded in 2008 in Ulm, Germany, and has grown into one of Germany's leading providers of applicant management and e-recruiting software. The company is owner-managed, holds ISO 27001 and ISO 9001 certifications, and emphasizes that all software development and data storage takes place exclusively in Germany. With over 6,500 customers and approximately 82 employees, BITE has remained independent throughout its history.
BITE.summary_abuse: BITE has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B HR recruiting software platform serving German and European employers, with no consumer-facing signup and no content-hosting or redirect features that would attract fraudsters. The company's strict German data residency and ISO certifications reflect a security-conscious posture.

BiteSpeed.history: BiteSpeed was founded in 2019 in Bengaluru, India, by Vinayak Aggarwal, Siddharth Barpanda, Abhinav Tuli, and Aparna Aggarwal. The company started as a Shopify side project focused on cart abandonment recovery via WhatsApp and Facebook Messenger. It raised a $1.9M seed round in 2022 led by Peak XV (formerly Sequoia Surge), followed by a $3.5M pre-Series A in January 2024. The company reached $10M ARR and claims to have powered over $1B in GMV for Shopify merchants.
BiteSpeed.summary_abuse: BiteSpeed is a marketing automation platform for Shopify merchants with no notable abuse history. It operates primarily as a WhatsApp and SMS marketing tool, where spam risk exists if merchants misuse messaging capabilities, but the platform targets legitimate ecommerce use cases and requires Shopify store ownership to onboard. No documented cases of the platform being used for phishing or fraud.

Bitmovin.history: Bitmovin was founded in 2013 as a spin-off from research at the University of Klagenfurt, Austria, by Christopher Müller, Stefan Lederer, and Christian Timmerer. The founders co-created the MPEG-DASH adaptive streaming standard used by YouTube and Netflix. The company went through Y Combinator's summer 2015 cohort and raised a $30M Series B in 2018. Bitmovin is headquartered in San Francisco with offices in Vienna, London, Berlin, and Denver, serving customers like BBC, fuboTV, and Hulu.
Bitmovin.summary_abuse: Bitmovin is a professional video streaming infrastructure provider serving major media companies. It has no notable abuse history. The platform requires enterprise contracts and is used by well-known broadcasters, making it an unlikely target for or enabler of abuse. No documented incidents of misuse for malware distribution or illicit content hosting.

BitPay.history: Founded in 2011 by Tony Gallippi and Stephen Pair in Atlanta, Georgia, as one of the first Bitcoin payment processors. Raised venture funding from Index Ventures, Founders Fund, and others. Obtained a BitLicense from the New York State Department of Financial Services in 2018. Expanded from Bitcoin-only to multi-cryptocurrency support including Ethereum, Litecoin, and stablecoins. No ownership changes or acquisitions to date.
BitPay.summary_abuse: BitPay maintains robust KYC/AML compliance as a regulated money services business and licensed money transmitter. Its published Acceptable Use Policy prohibits high-risk categories including drugs, weapons, gambling, and pyramid schemes. While any crypto payment processor is occasionally targeted by fraudulent merchants, BitPay enforces merchant vetting, transaction monitoring, and cooperates with law enforcement. No major public abuse incidents specific to BitPay have been documented. The platform does not offer anonymous signup or free-tier custom-domain hosting that fraudsters commonly exploit.

Bitrix24.history: Bitrix, Inc. was founded in 1998 in Russia by Sergey Ryzhikov, Dmitry Valyanov, Alexey Sidorenko, and others. Originally a web content management system, the company launched Bitrix24 as a cloud-based CRM and collaboration platform in April 2012. The company is incorporated in the United States (Alexandria, VA) with historical roots and large operations in Russia and Ukraine. With geopolitical concerns raised after Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, some enterprises reviewed their use of Bitrix24, though the company maintained US incorporation and EU data hosting options.
Bitrix24.summary_abuse: Bitrix24 has no major documented abuse incidents specific to its platform. However, its broad free tier and wide international user base including Russia and CIS countries means it is occasionally found in threat intelligence reports as a communication or data staging tool by threat actors. The vendor publishes an acceptable use policy and provides EU data storage options for GDPR compliance. Abuse potential is moderate due to free tier access and limited KYC requirements.

BittAds.history: BittAds is a small advertising network based in The Hague, Netherlands, with a Twitter presence dating to January 2010. The platform appears to serve display advertising. The website was unreachable at time of evaluation, suggesting the service may be dormant or discontinued.
BittAds.summary_abuse: BittAds is a small advertising network with limited public documentation. Small and obscure ad networks can be vectors for malvertising campaigns, where advertisers inject malicious scripts via ad creatives. The inaccessibility of the BittAds website and limited operational visibility make it difficult to assess the vendor's anti-abuse posture or AUP enforcement. Given the very small scale and limited visibility, its practical abuse impact is likely low.

BizSpring.history: BizSpring was established in 2002 in Seoul, South Korea. The company focuses on MarTech and AdTech solutions for online customer behavior analytics and marketing automation. Its flagship product BizSpring Insight is an enterprise-level web log analytics solution. The company has operated continuously for over two decades serving primarily the Korean enterprise market.
BizSpring.summary_abuse: BizSpring is a Korean enterprise analytics vendor with no documented abuse incidents. The platform collects web behavioral and marketing data for its enterprise clients, raising standard privacy considerations around user tracking, but is used exclusively in a B2B context and operates under Korean personal data protection laws.

Bizweb.history: Bizweb was founded in 2008 in Hanoi, Vietnam, as a cloud-based ecommerce platform for Vietnamese SMBs. It was part of the DKT Technology Group. In April 2018, Bizweb officially merged with Sapo, another DKT product, with the Sapo brand becoming the unified platform for website and omnichannel sales management software in Vietnam.
Bizweb.summary_abuse: Bizweb is a Vietnamese ecommerce platform primarily used by local SMBs with no documented abuse incidents. Operating in the Vietnamese market, it has a standard acceptable use policy for its merchants. Abuse risk is limited as the platform serves legitimate local retailers in a regulated domestic market.

Black Book.history: Black Book Market Research is a US-based healthcare-focused competitive intelligence and market research firm. It provides independent vendor ratings and technology evaluations for the healthcare IT sector, covering over 12,000 products from 4,000+ vendors across 110 countries. The company is known for its healthcare IT vendor rankings published annually.
Black Book.summary_abuse: Black Book Market Research is a healthcare IT research and ratings platform with no documented abuse incidents. The platform focuses on B2B market intelligence and vendor evaluation for healthcare technology buyers. It does not host user-generated content or provide infrastructure services that could be easily abused.

Black Crow.history: Black Crow AI was founded around 2020 and is a New York-based AI platform for ecommerce brands. The company builds predictive machine learning models trained on first-party commerce and behavioral data to help Shopify and other ecommerce merchants improve marketing performance. It offers a 30-day free trial and focuses on conversion rate optimization and customer acquisition.
Black Crow.summary_abuse: Black Crow AI is a predictive analytics platform for ecommerce merchants with no documented abuse incidents. The platform collects behavioral and purchase data from Shopify stores, which has standard first-party data privacy implications for end consumers. No documented instances of the platform being misused for fraud or unauthorized data collection.

Blackbaud CRM.history: Blackbaud was founded in 1981 by Anthony Bakker, who developed a computerized billing system for a school in Manhattan. The company relocated to Mount Pleasant, South Carolina in 1989. It became a publicly traded company (NASDAQ: BLKB) focused exclusively on nonprofit software, providing CRM, fundraising, finance, and analytics tools. Blackbaud suffered a significant ransomware attack in 2020 that exposed donor data from numerous nonprofits, resulting in regulatory penalties and class-action lawsuits.
Blackbaud CRM.summary_abuse: Blackbaud is a publicly traded nonprofit software company that suffered a major ransomware attack in May 2020. The attackers exfiltrated data before Blackbaud removed them, and Blackbaud paid a ransom but the stolen data was not certified as destroyed. This affected hundreds of nonprofit clients worldwide and led to FTC enforcement action and state attorney general settlements. The company has since improved its security posture. No pattern of ongoing abuse as a platform enabling external attackers.

Blackcart.history: Blackcart was founded by Donny Ouyang and raised an $8.8M Series A in January 2021 led by 500 Startups. The company pioneered try-before-you-buy commerce infrastructure, integrating with Shopify, Magento, WooCommerce, BigCommerce, and Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Blackcart charges retailers a revenue-share (4-10% of kept items) rather than a flat fee.
Blackcart.summary_abuse: Blackcart is a try-before-you-buy ecommerce platform with no documented abuse incidents. The business model involves deferred payments tied to kept items, creating some inherent fraud risk from consumers who take items and fail to return them or pay, which Blackcart manages with a fraud multiplier in pricing. The platform does not enable consumer-facing abuse scenarios typical of ad-tech or link shorteners.

Blade.history: Blade is the built-in templating engine for the Laravel PHP framework, created by Taylor Otwell when Laravel was first released in 2011. It uses .blade.php file extensions and compiles to plain PHP cached on disk. Blade is not an independent library but an integral part of Laravel, maintained by the Laravel team and the community under the MIT license.
Blade.summary_abuse: Blade is Laravel's templating engine and has no notable abuse history. As a backend PHP templating system, it has no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited for phishing or fraud. Security considerations are limited to standard template injection risks if user input is improperly rendered, which is mitigated by Blade's default HTML escaping of output.

Blankshield.history: Blankshield is a small open-source JavaScript library created by Daniel St. Jules to prevent reverse tabnabbing phishing attacks caused by target=_blank links. The library was later maintained by Massdrop. The last npm publish was over 7 years ago at version 0.6.3, indicating the project is no longer actively maintained.
Blankshield.summary_abuse: Blankshield is a security-focused client-side JavaScript library specifically designed to prevent phishing attacks, and has no abuse history of its own. The library helps mitigate the reverse tabnabbing vulnerability where pages opened via target=_blank can redirect the opener window.

BLAZE.history: BLAZE is a cannabis POS and retail management system founded around 2016, serving dispensaries and delivery services in the cannabis industry. No notable ownership changes documented.
BLAZE.summary_abuse: BLAZE has no notable abuse signal beyond operating in the regulated cannabis sector. It is a POS system for licensed dispensaries with no consumer-facing content hosting that would attract fraud. Cannabis sector regulatory compliance is the relevant risk consideration rather than platform abuse.

Blazor.history: Blazor was first unveiled by Microsoft engineer Steve Sanderson at NDC Oslo in 2017 as an experimental framework for building client-side web applications with C# and WebAssembly. It was admitted as an official open-source project by Microsoft. Blazor Server shipped with .NET Core 3.0 in 2018, and Blazor WebAssembly followed in 2020 with .NET 5. Blazor is part of ASP.NET Core and is maintained by Microsoft with active development continuing across server-side, WebAssembly, and hybrid modes.
Blazor.summary_abuse: Blazor is Microsoft's open-source web framework with no abuse history. As a server-side and client-side rendering framework, it has no consumer-facing surface susceptible to abuse beyond what any web application framework might expose. Microsoft maintains a responsible disclosure program and active security patching for the framework.

Bleckmann.history: Founded in 1862 by Benjamin Bleckmann as a transport company serving the textile industry in Oldenzaal, Netherlands. The modern company formed in 2014 through the merger of TNT Fashion (Dutch) and Belspeed (Belgian). Major shareholder is Netlog Logistics Group, whose co-founder Gokalp Cak serves as Chairman of the Board and interim CEO as of January 2026. Revenue grew from approximately 280 million in 2018 to around 730 million by 2025, with 7000 employees across 40+ sites in Europe and the US.
Bleckmann.summary_abuse: Bleckmann is a physical logistics and fulfillment provider for fashion and lifestyle brands with minimal digital abuse surface. As a 3PL operator handling warehousing, freight forwarding, and physical order fulfillment, it does not offer consumer-facing hosting, URL redirection, mass-email, or file-upload capabilities that fraudsters typically exploit. The website uses standard marketing cookies and lead-gen forms. No notable abuse incidents or documented abuse patterns are associated with the service. Their security contact is available via a valid security.txt file routed through a third-party security firm.

Blendee.history: Blendee is an Italian marketing automation and customer data platform that describes itself as Europe's first Marketing Operating System. Based in Milan, the platform combines CDP, ad server, and DMP capabilities for omnichannel marketing personalization. It was created through a merger within the Italian martech ecosystem, backed by Nana Bianca, and focuses primarily on the Italian and Southern European market.
Blendee.summary_abuse: Blendee is an Italian marketing personalization platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a CDP and marketing automation tool, it collects behavioral data about end users on behalf of its clients, which carries standard privacy obligations under GDPR. The platform is targeted at legitimate ecommerce and media brands.

Blendle.history: Blendle was founded in 2014 in the Netherlands by Marten Blankesteijn and Alexander Klöpping, backed by a Dutch government fund and later by a $3M investment from The New York Times Company and Axel Springer. It pioneered micropayment-based news article sales before pivoting to a premium subscription model (Blendle Premium) at around €10/month. In July 2020, Blendle was acquired by French news aggregator Cafeyn.
Blendle.summary_abuse: Blendle is a licensed news aggregation platform with formal publisher partnerships and no documented abuse incidents. The platform distributes paid journalism content and has strong incentives to prevent content piracy or redistribution abuse. It operates under Dutch law with GDPR compliance.

Blessing Skin.history: Blessing Skin is an open-source PHP web application created by a Chinese developer for managing custom Minecraft player skins on offline or private servers. The project is hosted on GitHub under the bs-community organization and is self-hosted by Minecraft server administrators. It has an active community around Chinese Minecraft server hosting.
Blessing Skin.summary_abuse: Blessing Skin is a self-hosted Minecraft skin management tool with no notable abuse history. It is used by Minecraft community operators to let players upload and share custom skins. As a self-hosted tool, abuse depends on the instance operator's policies. No documented cases of the platform being weaponized for malware distribution or fraud.

Blesta.history: Blesta was founded in 2007 in Los Angeles, California, as a billing and customer management system designed for web hosting companies. The platform is nearly 100% open with only three files encoded for license protection, and supports 30+ payment gateways and 40+ provisioning modules. It competes with WHMCS in the web hosting billing market.
Blesta.summary_abuse: Blesta is a web hosting billing platform used by hosting resellers and providers. Hosting providers using Blesta can themselves host malicious content or offer services to bad actors, making the indirect abuse surface meaningful. However, Blesta itself as a billing platform has no documented direct abuse history and is used predominantly by legitimate hosting businesses.

Blinger.history: Blinger.io is an omnichannel helpdesk platform that aggregates messaging apps including WhatsApp, Telegram, Instagram, and email into a single inbox. The company is based in Russia and integrates with major CRM and helpdesk platforms like Zendesk, Salesforce, and Freshdesk. The platform is used by businesses to centralize customer support across messaging channels.
Blinger.summary_abuse: Blinger.io is a customer support messaging aggregation platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a business-to-business tool used by customer service teams, it does not provide anonymous public-facing infrastructure that could be easily exploited for spam or phishing. The company's Russian origin warrants some caution for enterprises in GDPR-regulated environments.

BLiNK AI.history: BLiNK AI is an AI-powered service scheduling and marketing automation platform for automotive dealerships. Very limited public history available.
BLiNK AI.summary_abuse: BLiNK AI has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche AI scheduling tool for auto dealerships with no consumer-facing content hosting.

Blinklink.history: Blinklink is a video commerce platform headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, with backing from Carnegie Mellon University. The company enables enterprises to create Agentic Media Platforms combining personalized short video feeds with AI-powered customer experience capabilities including research, support, and execution. The platform supports embedded video feeds in web pages with adaptive streaming technology.
Blinklink.summary_abuse: Blinklink is a video commerce and enterprise media platform with no documented abuse incidents. As an enterprise-focused B2B product for video embedding and personalization, it does not offer anonymous public-facing infrastructure commonly exploited for spam or phishing. Standard data collection via video engagement analytics applies.

Blip.history: Blip (formerly Take) was founded in 2017 in Belo Horizonte, Brazil, as a conversational AI platform. The company raised $230M led by Warburg Pincus and SoftBank Group. It operates across Brazil, Mexico, and Spain and has transmitted over 80 billion messages across 32 countries. The platform serves enterprises seeking to automate WhatsApp, Instagram, and Telegram customer communications.
Blip.summary_abuse: Blip is a Brazilian enterprise conversational AI platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a B2B platform serving large brands for WhatsApp and messaging automation, it applies KYC requirements and operates under Meta's WhatsApp Business Platform policies, which limit bulk spam and unauthorized messaging. No documented misuse for phishing or fraud at scale.

Blippa.history: Blippa was founded around 2020 in Stockholm, Sweden by Mikael Runhem and Daniel Carlman as a platform for digital product passports and QR/NFC-based product lifecycle management. No notable ownership changes since founding.
Blippa.summary_abuse: Blippa is a niche B2B SaaS for digital product passports with no notable abuse signal. The platform manages product data and lifecycle tracking and has no consumer-facing content-hosting features relevant to scam or phishing operations.

Blitz.history: Blitz (blitz.gg) is a gaming companion application that provides in-game overlays, meta statistics, tier lists, and performance analytics for games like League of Legends, Valorant, and Fortnite. The company partnered with Databricks for data infrastructure to power its competitive gaming analytics. It operates a free tier with premium features.
Blitz.summary_abuse: Blitz is a gaming companion app with no major documented abuse incidents. As a gaming overlay and analytics tool used by individuals, it collects game performance data. Cheating software often resembles overlays, but Blitz positions itself as a legitimate meta-analysis tool. The platform installs a desktop client, which requires trust in the vendor's software supply chain.

Blitz.js.history: Blitz.js was created by Brandon Bayer in 2020 as a full-stack React framework inspired by Ruby on Rails, built on top of Next.js with a Zero-API data layer. The framework eliminates the need for REST or GraphQL API layers between frontend and backend. Blitz.js later pivoted its main product to the Blitz Toolkit, a set of utilities for Next.js, as the monolithic framework approach became less competitive against the evolving Next.js ecosystem.
Blitz.js.summary_abuse: Blitz.js is an open-source full-stack React framework with no abuse history. As a developer framework for building web applications, it has no consumer-facing surface susceptible to abuse. Applications built with Blitz.js are the responsibility of their respective developers.

Blockly.history: Blockly is Google's open-source JavaScript library for building visual block-based programming editors, first released in 2012. It powers major coding education platforms including Scratch and Code.org, serving tens of millions of students worldwide. Blockly is maintained by Google as part of its education and developer tools portfolio and is released under the Apache 2.0 license.
Blockly.summary_abuse: Blockly is a Google-maintained open-source visual programming library with no abuse history. As a purely client-side JavaScript library for building educational coding editors, it has no network surface susceptible to abuse. It is used extensively in legitimate coding education products.

Blocksy.history: Blocksy is a WordPress theme created around 2019 by Sergiu and Andrei, two developers from the Republic of Moldova operating as CreativeThemes. The free theme launched approximately two years before Blocksy Pro in October 2020. By mid-2021 the theme was active on over 20,000 WordPress sites with no ownership changes or notable controversies.
Blocksy.summary_abuse: Blocksy is a WordPress theme with no documented abuse incidents. As a self-hosted WordPress theme, abuse depends on the end user's hosting environment and how they use it. The theme itself does not collect end-user data or include tracking code that would raise privacy concerns.

Blocksy Companion.history: Blocksy Companion is the official companion plugin for the Blocksy WordPress theme, developed by CreativeThemes (Sergiu and Andrei from the Republic of Moldova). It extends the Blocksy theme with additional features like starter sites, custom hooks, and third-party plugin integrations. The companion plugin was released alongside Blocksy Pro in October 2020.
Blocksy Companion.summary_abuse: Blocksy Companion is a WordPress plugin companion to the Blocksy theme with no documented abuse incidents. As a self-hosted WordPress plugin that extends theme functionality, it does not collect user data independently and has no consumer-facing surface susceptible to abuse.

Blogger.history: Blogger was founded in 1999 by Evan Williams and Meg Hourihan at Pyra Labs as one of the earliest hosted blogging services. Google acquired Pyra Labs and Blogger in February 2003. Blogger popularized blogging as a mainstream activity and allowed users to host blogs at blogspot.com subdomains for free. Google has maintained but not significantly developed Blogger since the mid-2010s as attention shifted to other products.
Blogger.summary_abuse: Blogger has a significant and well-documented abuse history. The free hosting service with no-KYC signup and custom subdomain support has been consistently used for phishing pages, malware distribution, spam blogs (splogs), scam landing pages, and fake product sites. Google's automated abuse detection removes a large volume of malicious content, but the platform's free anonymous access continues to attract bad actors. The blogspot.com domain appears frequently in phishing and malware threat intelligence feeds.

BlogHunch.history: BlogHunch was founded in 2022 in Kolkata, West Bengal, India, as a no-code blogging platform targeting creators and businesses looking to build SEO-optimized blogs without technical overhead. The company operates in the media and entertainment sector and serves over 8,000 businesses. It is bootstrapped with 11-50 employees.
BlogHunch.summary_abuse: BlogHunch is a hosted blogging platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a content hosting platform, there is inherent risk of spam blogs or SEO manipulation being published by users, which is a common challenge for all hosted blogging platforms. The company is small and their content moderation capacity is limited.

BlokID.history: BlokID was founded in July 2023 and raised a $1.25M seed round in January 2024 led by Ascend Vietnam Ventures and AppWorks. The company provides blockchain-based advertising attribution and audit technology, helping advertisers verify ad spend and detect attribution fraud. The company later rebranded to Vaudit.
BlokID.summary_abuse: BlokID (now Vaudit) is an advertising attribution audit platform designed to protect advertisers from fraud, not to enable it. The technology itself is focused on detecting abuse in digital advertising rather than enabling it. No documented cases of the platform being used for malicious purposes.

Bloom.history: Bloom is an email opt-in and lead generation WordPress plugin developed by Elegant Themes, the company behind the popular Divi page builder. Bloom was created as a premium plugin to help WordPress site owners grow their email lists through customizable pop-up and inline opt-in forms. It integrates with 19 major email marketing services and is included in Elegant Themes' all-access membership.
Bloom.summary_abuse: Bloom is a WordPress email opt-in plugin by Elegant Themes with no documented abuse incidents. As a self-hosted WordPress plugin for collecting email subscriptions, its abuse potential is limited to misuse of collected email addresses by site operators. The plugin itself does not operate any shared infrastructure that could be exploited.

Bloom Labs.history: Bloom Labs is a local content targeting platform headquartered in New York, NY, focused on helping publishers distribute news content based on geographic relevance. The company addresses the challenges of local journalism by providing geo-tagging and location-aware content distribution tools for newsrooms. It remains a small independent company.
Bloom Labs.summary_abuse: Bloom Labs is a niche local news distribution platform with no documented abuse signal. Its geographic content targeting system serves legitimate news publishers and has no anonymous signup or consumer-facing content hosting that would attract phishing or fraud campaigns.

Bloom Portfolio.history: Bloom Portfolio is the portfolio website feature within the Bloom.io all-in-one CRM and business management platform designed primarily for photographers and freelancers. Bloom.io was founded around 2014 and provides tools for contracts, invoicing, scheduling, and client galleries, with the portfolio feature allowing creatives to showcase their work online.
Bloom Portfolio.summary_abuse: Bloom Portfolio is a portfolio hosting feature within the Bloom.io freelancer CRM with no documented abuse incidents. As a photography portfolio hosting service for credentialed creative professionals, the risk of abuse is low. The platform primarily hosts professional portfolios for paying subscribers.

Bloomreach.history: Bloomreach was founded in 2009 by Raj De Datta and Ashutosh Garg in Mountain View, California. It started as a big data platform for e-commerce SEO, raised $5M from Bain Capital Ventures, and expanded internationally by 2014. In 2016 it acquired Hippo CMS to pivot to a full digital experience platform. The $300M+ acquisition of Exponea in 2021 added CDP capabilities. By 2025 Bloomreach exceeded $260M in ARR with its AI platform Loomi.
Bloomreach.summary_abuse: Bloomreach is an enterprise digital experience platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a B2B platform serving major retail and ecommerce brands, it operates behind enterprise contracts and does not offer anonymous public-facing infrastructure that could be exploited for spam or phishing.

Bloomreach Discovery.history: Bloomreach Discovery is the search and product discovery module within the Bloomreach Digital Experience Platform, incorporating AI-powered site search, merchandising, and product recommendation capabilities. It grew from Bloomreach's original e-commerce SEO platform and was substantially enhanced through the Exponea CDP acquisition in 2021, integrating behavioral data for personalized search ranking.
Bloomreach Discovery.summary_abuse: Bloomreach Discovery is an enterprise e-commerce search and merchandising product with no documented abuse incidents. As a B2B solution sold to major retailers and deployed on their storefronts, it does not provide any public-facing infrastructure susceptible to abuse.

Blossom Travel.history: Blossom Travel is a travel agency and concierge services company based in Greece, operating under Kennedy's Group founded by CEO Elpida Kennedy. The company provides luxury villa rental, property management, travel management, event management, and real estate services primarily in the Greek and Mediterranean market.
Blossom Travel.summary_abuse: Blossom Travel is a small travel and concierge services company with no documented technology abuse incidents. As a traditional travel agency with a web presence, the platform does not expose significant abuse vectors beyond standard web fraud risks. Very limited public information is available about the company.

Blotout EdgeTag.history: Blotout was founded in 2019 by Alex Stieb and Mandar Shinde and is based in California. The company is Y Combinator-backed and raised seed funding from Y Combinator, First Rays Venture Partners, and others. Its flagship product EdgeTag uses Cloudflare Workers to run first-party tracking at the CDN edge, helping advertisers recover signal lost to iOS privacy changes and cookieless browsers.
Blotout EdgeTag.summary_abuse: Blotout EdgeTag is a privacy-first first-party data infrastructure tool designed to help advertisers recover ad attribution signal without third-party cookies. The platform is explicitly anti-tracking in the traditional sense, running in the advertiser's own CDN account rather than on shared infrastructure. No documented abuse incidents.

Bludit.history: Bludit is an open-source flat-file CMS written in PHP and released under the MIT license. The project stores content as JSON files rather than using a database, making it simple to deploy and migrate. It was created around 2015 and is maintained by its original developer. The project had a known vulnerability (CVE-2019-14287) that allowed brute-force bypass, which was patched. It has limited active development momentum in recent years.
Bludit.summary_abuse: Bludit is a self-hosted flat-file CMS with a historical vulnerability (CVE-2019-14287) that allowed brute-force login bypass. This vulnerability was patched, but sites running outdated versions remain exposed. As a self-hosted tool, abuse depends on how individual sites are maintained. No documented cases of the CMS being used as a malware distribution platform beyond individual compromised installations.

Blue.history: Blue (getblue.io) is a digital retargeting and ecommerce data marketing company based in Brazil with offices in Spain and Argentina. The company provides programmatic retargeting using machine learning and AI, focused on recovering cart abandoners and lost website visitors through display advertising. It operates in 50+ countries and claims to build all technology in-house.
Blue.summary_abuse: Blue is a retargeting and ad-tech platform. Ad-tech companies by nature track user browsing behavior across sites, which raises privacy concerns. Blue emphasizes GDPR compliance and first-party data, claiming not to use third-party intermediaries. As a retargeting network, its display inventory could in theory carry misleading or deceptive ads, though no specific documented abuse incidents have been publicly reported.

Blue Corona.history: Blue Corona was founded in 2008 in Gaithersburg, Maryland, as a digital marketing agency specializing in home service businesses (HVAC, plumbing, electrical). The company grew to become one of Inc. magazine's fastest-growing private companies multiple years running. In October 2024, Blue Corona merged with RYNO Strategic Solutions to form a combined home services digital marketing agency.
Blue Corona.summary_abuse: Blue Corona is a digital marketing agency serving home service businesses with no documented abuse incidents. As a professional services agency managing ad campaigns and SEO for legitimate contractors, it has no consumer-facing infrastructure susceptible to independent abuse.

Blue Triangle.history: Blue Triangle is a US-based digital experience management platform that correlates website performance with business outcomes such as revenue and conversion rates. The company provides real user monitoring, synthetic monitoring, and predictive analytics. It is recognized in Gartner Peer Insights for website performance monitoring.
Blue Triangle.summary_abuse: Blue Triangle is a digital experience monitoring platform used by enterprise e-commerce companies with no documented abuse incidents. As a JavaScript tag deployed on client websites for performance monitoring, the platform collects page performance metrics and user behavior data, with standard privacy implications for end-user tracking.

bluebarry.history: bluebarry is an AI-powered product recommendation and customer retention platform for ecommerce, operating at bluebarry.ai; no founding year or notable ownership history found in public records.
bluebarry.summary_abuse: bluebarry is a personalization and product recommendation SaaS for ecommerce. It collects shopper behavior data to drive recommendations, which is standard marketing-platform use. No notable abuse patterns or security incidents have been documented.

BlueConic.history: BlueConic was founded in 2010 in the Netherlands as a pure-play customer data platform. The company expanded to Boston and built a global customer base including Heineken, ING, T-Mobile Netherlands, Forbes, and Michelin. In 2022 it received a strategic investment from Vista Equity Partners. BlueConic was named a Strong Performer in the 2024 Forrester Wave for CDPs.
BlueConic.summary_abuse: BlueConic is an enterprise CDP with no documented abuse incidents. As a B2B platform serving large consumer brands for first-party customer data management, it operates under enterprise contracts with GDPR compliance requirements. The platform processes large volumes of consumer profile data on behalf of clients, which requires robust data handling practices.

Bluecore.history: Bluecore was founded in 2013 in New York by former retail marketing executives. The company built a retail-specific marketing platform that combines shopper, behavioral, and product catalog data to power personalized email and SMS campaigns. It serves 400+ brands including Tapestry, Lenovo, Teleflora, and Alo Yoga, and recently launched an AI-powered Marketing Agent product.
Bluecore.summary_abuse: Bluecore is a retail marketing platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a B2B platform serving major retail brands for email and SMS personalization, it operates under enterprise contracts with data processing agreements. The platform collects consumer behavior data on behalf of retailer clients, which requires GDPR and CCPA compliance.

Bluefish.history: Bluefish is a free, open-source text editor for programmers and web developers, originally created in 1997. It runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows and is hosted on SourceForge. The editor supports syntax highlighting for dozens of programming and markup languages. Bluefish is released under the GPL and is maintained by a small volunteer community.
Bluefish.summary_abuse: Bluefish is a desktop text editor with no documented abuse history. As a locally installed software tool, it has no network-facing surface susceptible to abuse. No documented security incidents related to the software itself.

Bluehost.history: Bluehost was conceived by Matt Heaton in 1996 and officially founded in 2003 with Danny Ashworth. It grew to become one of the world's largest WordPress hosting providers. In 2010, Bluehost was acquired by Endurance International Group (EIG). EIG later rebranded to NewFold Digital, which continues to own Bluehost. Bluehost has been an officially recommended WordPress host since 2005.
Bluehost.summary_abuse: Bluehost is a major shared web hosting provider with a documented history of hosting spam and phishing sites. Shared hosting providers are commonly abused because of low-cost plans and limited proactive content monitoring. Bluehost has AUP enforcement and abuse reporting but shared hosting abuse is an industry-wide challenge. The blogspot and shared hosting infrastructure is a common vector for phishing campaigns and malware distribution by bad actors who compromise or create accounts.

Blueknow.history: Blueknow was founded in 2009 in Barcelona, Spain, by Francesc Magdaleno, Santiago Ameller, and Lino Bort. The company provides ecommerce recommendation engine and cart abandonment recovery services, operating as a SaaS platform for mid-to-large ecommerce businesses, travel agencies, universities, and telecoms. The company raised $854K in two funding rounds and generates approximately $1.4M in annual revenue with 17 employees.
Blueknow.summary_abuse: Blueknow is a Spanish ecommerce personalization and cart abandonment platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a B2B tool used by e-commerce retailers to send personalized recommendations and recovery emails, the platform's email sending capability carries standard spam risk if misused by clients. No documented cases of the platform itself enabling fraud.

Blueshift.history: Blueshift was founded in 2014 in San Francisco, California. The company pioneered what it calls the Customer Data Activation Platform (CDAP), combining CDP, predictive AI, and cross-channel marketing automation. Blueshift is SOC2 and HIPAA compliant and serves brands across retail, media, and financial services verticals with email, SMS, push, and in-app channel orchestration.
Blueshift.summary_abuse: Blueshift is an enterprise customer data and marketing automation platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a B2B platform serving businesses for customer engagement, it operates under data processing agreements and maintains SOC2 and HIPAA compliance. No documented cases of platform misuse for spam or phishing.

Bluestone PIM.history: Bluestone PIM was established in 2016 in Tønsberg, Norway, with the goal of building a composable, API-first, cloud-native Product Information Management system aligned with MACH architecture principles. The company has expanded to offices in Stockholm, Amsterdam, Palo Alto, Gdansk, and Bangkok, with 100 employees across 4 countries. It is recognized as a Representative Vendor in the 2025 Gartner Market Guide for PIM Solutions.
Bluestone PIM.summary_abuse: Bluestone PIM is an enterprise product information management platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a B2B backend data management system for product catalogs, it has no consumer-facing surface susceptible to abuse.

Blutui.history: Blutui was founded in 2017 in Hamilton, New Zealand, and is a Kiwi-owned and operated cloud platform designed for digital agencies to build, deliver, and manage web projects. The platform name is inspired by the tui, a native New Zealand bird. It provides an agency-focused web development environment with in-page editing and client management tools at $25/month per published site.
Blutui.summary_abuse: Blutui is a New Zealand web platform used by digital agencies with no documented abuse incidents. As a paid subscription platform for professional agencies, its barrier to entry reduces abuse risk compared to free hosting platforms. No documented cases of the platform being used for malicious content hosting.

BMT Micro.history: BMT Micro has been providing e-commerce order fulfillment and payment processing for software vendors since 1994. It is one of the oldest digital goods e-commerce service providers. No notable ownership changes documented.
BMT Micro.summary_abuse: BMT Micro is a long-established digital commerce and order fulfillment service. It processes payments for software vendors and carries standard payment fraud risks. No documented pattern of specific abuse targeting BMT Micro infrastructure.

Bnovo.history: Bnovo was founded in 2012 in Russia as a cloud-based property management system for hotels, hostels, apartments, and accommodation facilities. The company serves over 8,000 end-users across Russia, Europe, CIS countries, and Asia. It integrates with 30+ OTA channels including Booking.com and Airbnb.
Bnovo.summary_abuse: Bnovo is a Russian hotel management software provider with no documented abuse incidents specifically related to its platform. As a property management system, it handles booking data and payments. The company's Russian origin warrants review under applicable data sovereignty and sanctions compliance requirements for international hotel clients.

BoatChat.history: BoatChat is a niche live-chat service based in Tarpon Springs, Florida, built specifically for marine and boat dealerships. No notable ownership changes or acquisitions are documented.
BoatChat.summary_abuse: BoatChat is a niche live-chat SaaS for boat dealerships with no notable abuse signal. The service is targeted at a specific vertical with no anonymous or consumer-facing content hosting features.

Boats Group.history: Boats Group formed in 2010 by consolidating Dominion Enterprises marine assets, including Boat Trader, YachtWorld, and boats.com. In July 2016 it was acquired by Apax Partners, and in February 2021 ownership transferred to Permira, a global private equity firm.
Boats Group.summary_abuse: Boats Group operates marine marketplace listings and has minimal abuse signal. Occasional fraudulent boat listings or scam inquiries can appear on marketplace platforms generally, but Boats Group is a legitimate industry operator with no documented systemic abuse issues.

Boba.js.history: Boba.js is a small, lightweight JavaScript utility library for event tracking with Google Analytics, created by digital agency space150. It was published as an open-source MIT-licensed library on GitHub. The project appears to have been abandoned or archived and is no longer actively maintained, reflecting its niche use case as a thin Google Analytics helper wrapper.
Boba.js.summary_abuse: No abuse signals. Boba.js is a simple, transparent Google Analytics helper library. It is open source with MIT license, allowing full code inspection. No malicious behavior, hidden tracking, or abuse patterns reported.

Boberdoo.history: Boberdoo was founded in 2000 by Brad Seiler in Chicago, Illinois, specializing in lead distribution software, ping-post systems, and call routing for the lead generation industry. The company has remained privately held and bootstrapped throughout its history.
Boberdoo.summary_abuse: Boberdoo is a lead distribution and ping-post platform for the lead generation industry. Lead distribution systems carry elevated abuse risk because they facilitate the buying and selling of consumer data and inquiries. Boberdoo itself does not generate leads but routes them between buyers and sellers, and unscrupulous lead buyers or fraudulent leads can flow through the system. The platform has no documented large-scale abuse incidents but the nature of lead brokering is inherently higher risk than typical SaaS.

Bobgo.history: Bob Go (Bobgo) was founded in 2012 in South Africa, originally under the brand uAfrica, as a shipping aggregator connecting South African e-commerce businesses to local courier networks. Over the following decade the company rebranded to Bob Go and expanded its order management capabilities, serving thousands of South African online retailers by 2022. It remains headquartered in Tshwane (Pretoria), South Africa.
Bobgo.summary_abuse: Bob Go has no notable abuse signal. It is a logistics and shipping aggregation platform serving legitimate South African e-commerce merchants. The service handles order fulfillment coordination rather than content hosting or financial transactions, limiting its abuse surface.

Bobonus.history: Bobonus is operated by Sapna Oy, a Finnish company, and provides an ecommerce and ordering platform for restaurants. No notable ownership changes or founding date are publicly documented.
Bobonus.summary_abuse: Bobonus is a niche restaurant ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. The service is used by restaurants for online ordering and reservations, with no consumer-facing content hosting or anonymous signup features linked to fraud.

Bodygram.history: Bodygram was founded in January 2019 in Delaware, USA, developing AI-powered body measurement technology using smartphone photos. The company raised over $17M in funding and holds 14 US patents. It serves B2B clients in apparel, healthcare, and insurance sectors.
Bodygram.summary_abuse: Bodygram is an AI body measurement API and platform with no notable abuse signal. The service processes body images for B2B clients and is not a consumer-facing platform with content hosting or signup features that scammers exploit.

Boei.history: Boei is a Dutch lead generation chat widget tool founded in 2021 and headquartered in Utrecht, Netherlands. The product allows website owners to embed a chat button that connects visitors to messaging apps like WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, and similar platforms. It was built as a simple, privacy-friendly alternative to complex live chat solutions. Boei operates as a small independent or solo-founder SaaS product with transparent pricing.
Boei.summary_abuse: No abuse signals found for Boei. The platform is privacy-conscious, GDPR-compliant (operating from the Netherlands), and does not itself store chat messages since conversations route to third-party messaging apps. Low tracker density and no dark patterns identified. Suitable for privacy-aware deployments.

Bogos.history: Bogos is a Shopify app for creating BOGO (Buy One Get One) and free gift promotions, developed by Secomapp, a Vietnamese Shopify app development company. The app has grown to serve over 82,900 stores and is one of the more popular gift-with-purchase promotion tools in the Shopify ecosystem. Secomapp has developed multiple Shopify apps and the Bogos product has been continuously maintained and expanded with new promotional features.
Bogos.summary_abuse: No significant abuse signals found. Bogos is a standard e-commerce promotion app with a legitimate business model. It operates within the Shopify ecosystem and must comply with Shopify's partner requirements. The BOGO/free gift mechanism could theoretically be used for dark pattern promotions but this is at the merchant's discretion, not built into the tool. No fraud or data abuse reports found.

BoidCMS.history: BoidCMS is an open-source flat-file CMS written in PHP, created by developer Shuaib Yusuf Shuaib. The project is hosted on GitHub and licensed under the MIT License. It is designed as a lightweight content management system that stores data in flat files rather than a database, making it simple to deploy. The project was started around 2022 and targets developers who want a minimalist CMS without database dependencies.
BoidCMS.summary_abuse: No abuse signals. BoidCMS is a transparent open-source project with MIT license, allowing full code review. It is a small flat-file CMS with a minimal attack surface. No security incidents, malicious behavior, or abuse patterns reported. As with any self-hosted CMS, security depends on proper deployment and maintenance by the end user.

BokaBord.history: BokaBord is a Swedish restaurant table booking platform operated by BokaBord Nordic AB, connecting diners with restaurants across Sweden. The platform partners with restaurant management systems like WaiterAid. No notable ownership changes are documented.
BokaBord.summary_abuse: BokaBord is a restaurant reservation platform for the Swedish market with no notable abuse signal. The service facilitates table bookings and does not expose content hosting or anonymous features that fraudsters target.

Bokeh.history: Bokeh is an open-source Python interactive visualization library created around 2012-2013. It targets modern web browsers and enables the construction of elegant, versatile interactive plots and dashboards. Bokeh is a Sponsored Project of NumFOCUS, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit charity in the United States, giving it strong organizational backing and sustainability. The project is hosted on GitHub with a large community of contributors and has become one of the standard Python data visualization tools alongside Matplotlib and Plotly.
Bokeh.summary_abuse: No abuse signals. Bokeh is a well-established open-source Python data visualization library under NumFOCUS sponsorship with BSD license. The codebase is fully transparent and widely audited by the data science community. No security incidents, malicious behavior, or supply chain attacks reported.

Bokun.history: Bokun is a booking management software platform for tour and activity providers, founded in 2012 in Reykjavik, Iceland. The company built a comprehensive reservation management system covering inventory, channel management, and online booking for tours and experiences. In April 2018, TripAdvisor acquired Bokun, integrating it into its Experiences business to strengthen its tour operator technology offering. Under TripAdvisor ownership, Bokun has continued expanding its market reach globally.
Bokun.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals. Bokun is a legitimate tour booking platform owned by TripAdvisor, a publicly listed company. As part of TripAdvisor, it benefits from established privacy policies and compliance frameworks. Moderate tracker density is expected given TripAdvisor's advertising ecosystem. No fraud or dark pattern reports found.

Bold Brain.history: Bold Brain is an AI-powered product recommendation Shopify app developed by Bold Commerce, a Canadian e-commerce technology company founded in 2012 in Winnipeg by Yvan and Eric Boisjoli, Jay Myers, and Stefan Maynard. Bold Commerce grew into one of Shopify's largest app developers, raising over $161M USD in venture funding. Bold Brain uses machine learning to provide personalized product recommendations for Shopify merchants.
Bold Brain.summary_abuse: No abuse signals. Bold Brain is a product recommendation tool by Bold Commerce, an established Canadian e-commerce company. As a Shopify app, it must meet Shopify's partner requirements. No reports of data abuse, dark patterns, or malicious behavior. Standard e-commerce analytics data collection expected.

Bold Bundles.history: Bold Bundles is a Shopify app developed by Bold Commerce, a Canadian e-commerce technology company founded in 2012 in Winnipeg, Manitoba by Yvan and Eric Boisjoli, Jay Myers, and Stefan Maynard. Bold Commerce became one of the largest Shopify app developers, raising over CAD 161M in venture funding. Bold Bundles is part of the Bold Commerce suite of e-commerce optimization tools for Shopify merchants.
Bold Bundles.summary_abuse: No abuse signals. Bold Bundles is developed by Bold Commerce, an established Canadian company and certified Shopify partner. As a Shopify app, it adheres to Shopify's app review requirements. No reports of data misuse, dark patterns, or malicious behavior.

The company grew into one of Shopify's largest app development partners, serving over 760,000 merchants, and later expanded into composable checkout technology.

BoldGrid.history: BoldGrid is a WordPress-based website builder and suite of tools designed to simplify building and managing WordPress websites. The project launched in 2015 and is headquartered in Virginia Beach, Virginia. BoldGrid has received investment from Staenberg Venture Partners, Web Ventures, Resolution Ventures, and Potter Ventures. It was built by the same team that created W3 Total Cache and Easy SEO WordPress plugins. BoldGrid offers themes, page builders, backup solutions, and SEO tools under an open-core GPL model.
BoldGrid.summary_abuse: No significant abuse signals. BoldGrid is a GPL-licensed WordPress website builder with transparent open-source code. No reports of malicious behavior, data misuse, or security incidents. Standard WordPress plugin security practices apply. Risk profile is low.

Bolt CMS.history: Bolt CMS is an open-source PHP content management system developed by Two Kings, a Dutch web development agency, with first release in 2012. It is built on Symfony components and Silex, uses Twig templating, and supports SQLite, MySQL, or PostgreSQL databases. Bolt was developed as a developer-friendly CMS offering a clean, structured approach to content management with YAML-defined content types. The project provides RESTful and GraphQL APIs enabling headless CMS usage.
Bolt CMS.summary_abuse: No abuse signals. Bolt CMS is a transparent, open-source PHP CMS with clean architecture. The codebase is publicly auditable on GitHub. No security incidents or malicious behavior reported. Self-hosted deployment gives administrators full control. Low risk profile.

Bolt Payments.history: Bolt Financial was founded in 2014 in San Francisco by Stanford students Ryan Breslow and Eric Feldman. The company developed a one-click checkout technology designed to help independent retailers compete with Amazon's patented one-click purchasing. Bolt raised nearly $1 billion in total funding including a $355M Series E in December 2021 and a $399M Series D in October 2021, at a peak valuation of $11 billion. In early 2022 Ryan Breslow departed as CEO following a controversial Twitter campaign targeting Y Combinator and Stripe. He returned as CEO in March 2025 amid ongoing restructuring.
Bolt Payments.summary_abuse: Bolt has faced controversy due to its founder's public attacks on competitors and reporting of internal governance issues. While the product itself (checkout technology) has a standard risk profile, the company's turbulent leadership history and allegations of misrepresenting network size raised concerns. No confirmed data breaches or direct consumer harm reported. PCI DSS compliant as a payments company. Risk is moderate due to reputational and governance issues.

Bomb Bomb.history: BombBomb is a video email and messaging platform founded in 2006 by Darin Dawson and Conor McCluskey, based in Colorado Springs, Colorado. The platform enables users to record and send video emails for more personal business communication, and has found particular traction in the real estate industry. BombBomb serves over 40,000 customers across 43 countries. The company has raised approximately $2.93M in funding from Lighter Capital and REACH. It has remained a focused independent company targeting relationship-driven sales professionals.
Bomb Bomb.summary_abuse: No significant abuse signals. BombBomb is a legitimate video email platform used primarily in real estate and sales. As a video communication tool, it handles video recordings and email delivery. Email-tracking features (open/click tracking) are standard in sales tools. No reports of data misuse, spam facilitation, or malicious behavior.

BON Loyalty.history: BON Loyalty is a Shopify loyalty and referral program app founded around 2021. No notable ownership changes documented.
BON Loyalty.summary_abuse: BON Loyalty has no notable abuse signal. It is a Shopify loyalty program app with no consumer-facing content hosting that would attract fraudsters.

Bonfire.history: Bonfire (originally Bonfire Funds) was founded in 2012 as a custom apparel and fundraising platform, headquartered in Glen Allen, Virginia. The company allows individuals, nonprofits, and content creators to design and sell custom merchandise on-demand without inventory risk. In 2016, Bonfire relaunched at Bonfire.com to expand beyond pure fundraising to general custom merchandise. The Women's March on Washington 2017 was one of the company's most successful early campaigns. By 2019 the company had grown to nearly 200 employees.
Bonfire.summary_abuse: No significant abuse signals. Bonfire is a print-on-demand platform with a good reputation in the fundraising and creator community. No reports of scam merchandise, data misuse, or deceptive practices. Standard e-commerce data collection. Low risk profile.

Bonsai.history: Bonsai (Hello Bonsai) was founded in 2015 by Matt Brown and Matt Nish in San Francisco, California. The platform provides freelancers and small agencies with an all-in-one business management suite covering contracts, proposals, invoicing, CRM, and project management. In November 2025, Bonsai was acquired by Zoom Communications, signaling integration into Zoom's broader productivity and business tools ecosystem.
Bonsai.summary_abuse: No abuse signals. Bonsai is a reputable freelancer business management platform with a clean track record. No reports of data misuse, deceptive practices, or privacy violations. Now under Zoom Communications ownership, subject to Zoom's broader privacy practices.

Bontii.history: Bontii is a Danish SaaS company based in Skødstrup providing subscription and loyalty tools for webshop owners. No notable ownership changes or public funding rounds are documented.
Bontii.summary_abuse: Bontii is a niche Danish e-commerce subscription and loyalty platform with no notable abuse signal. The service is targeted at webshop operators with no consumer-facing anonymous signup features.

Book N Pay.history: Book N Pay is a hospitality booking and channel management platform based in Bali, Indonesia, operating as a subsidiary of PT. Kirana Bali Wisata. It serves hotels, villas, and activity providers primarily in the Indonesian market.
Book N Pay.summary_abuse: Book N Pay is a niche hospitality booking platform targeting Indonesian hotels and activity operators. It has no documented abuse signal; its use case is legitimate B2B SaaS for accommodation providers and it does not offer features commonly exploited for phishing or fraud.

Bookafy.history: Bookafy is an online appointment scheduling and booking software platform, founded circa 2014 in the United States. The company provides scheduling tools for service businesses and professionals, integrating with calendars, video conferencing, and CRM systems. It competes with Calendly and Acuity Scheduling in the appointment booking market. Bookafy offers a free tier and paid plans for growing businesses.
Bookafy.summary_abuse: No significant abuse signals. Bookafy is a standard online scheduling tool with transparent pricing. No reports of data misuse or malicious behavior. Standard contact data collection for scheduling purposes.

Bookatable.history: Bookatable was founded in 2006 as Livebookings by Christopher Persson and James Barnard in London. Michelin acquired the company in January 2016, and in December 2019 Michelin sold it to TheFork, a TripAdvisor subsidiary, consolidating the European restaurant reservation market.
Bookatable.summary_abuse: Bookatable is a restaurant reservation platform with no notable abuse signal. As part of TripAdvisor's TheFork ecosystem it maintains standard trust and safety practices, and its booking flows do not expose anonymous content hosting features.

BookBanket.history: BookBanket is a Russian banquet and event venue management SaaS. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
BookBanket.summary_abuse: BookBanket is a niche Russian banquet management SaaS with no documented abuse signal. The platform serves event venues and has no consumer-facing content hosting features relevant to fraud.

Bookboost.history: Bookboost was founded in 2016 by Willem Rabsztyn and Daan De Bruijn in Sweden as a hospitality CRM and guest messaging platform. The company raised EUR 3.6M in funding and was recognized at the 2019 World Travel Awards as a leading guest messaging platform.
Bookboost.summary_abuse: Bookboost is a hotel CRM and guest messaging SaaS with no notable abuse signal. The platform serves hospitality operators and has no consumer-facing anonymous content hosting features.

BookDinners.history: BookDinners is a Dutch social restaurant reservation platform developed in partnership with the Royal Hospitality Netherlands (Koninklijke Horeca Nederland). In 2018 it was repositioned as a social restaurant marketing platform combining reservations with social recommendations.
BookDinners.summary_abuse: BookDinners is a Dutch restaurant reservation platform with no notable abuse signal. The service facilitates table bookings for verified restaurants and has no anonymous content hosting features.

Booked.history: Booked is an open-source PHP scheduling and resource management system. It has been available for self-hosting for many years and offers a hosted cloud option. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Booked.summary_abuse: Booked is an open-source scheduling tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hostable backend scheduling system without consumer-facing anonymous content hosting features.

Bookeo.history: Bookeo was founded in 2010 by Giovanna Roncali in Bondi, Australia, offering online booking software for tours, classes, appointments, and events. It has grown to over 40,000 customers while remaining privately held and independent.
Bookeo.summary_abuse: Bookeo is a small-business booking SaaS with no notable abuse signal. The platform handles appointment and tour reservations and has no anonymous content hosting or free-tier features commonly exploited for fraud.

Booker.history: Booker Software was founded in 2007 by Peter Ellis and Daniel Lizio-Katzen as SpaBooker, a division of SpaFinder, providing cloud-based spa and salon management software. The company rebranded as Booker and grew to serve thousands of spas and salons with online booking, scheduling, and payment features. In March 2018, MINDBODY acquired Booker for approximately $150 million, adding around 10,000 salons and spas to the MINDBODY marketplace. In June 2025, Booker was unified with MINDBODY and ClassPass under the Playlist brand.
Booker.summary_abuse: No significant abuse signals. Booker is a legitimate spa and salon management platform now owned by MINDBODY, a well-established wellness software company. No reports of data misuse or deceptive practices. Standard business data collection for appointment scheduling and payments.

Bookero.history: Bookero was founded around 2020 in Swindon, United Kingdom, as a booking management SaaS targeting local businesses in automotive, healthcare, and beauty sectors. Tracxn classifies it as deadpooled.
Bookero.summary_abuse: Bookero is a small booking management SaaS with no notable abuse signal. The platform served local business appointment bookings and has no documented fraud or phishing exposure.

Booking Experts.history: Booking Experts was founded in 2011 in Overijssel, Netherlands by Ali Ilboga, evolving from Expert Webdesign (2002) into a cloud-based reservation system for holiday parks and campsites. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Booking Experts.summary_abuse: Booking Experts is a Dutch vacation rental PMS with no notable abuse signal. The platform is B2B focused for holiday park operators and has no consumer-facing anonymous content hosting features.

Booking Factory.history: Booking Factory was founded in 2014 in London, UK as a cloud-based all-in-one hotel management and booking engine system for independent hotels and venues. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Booking Factory.summary_abuse: Booking Factory is a niche hotel PMS and booking engine with no notable abuse signal. The platform is B2B focused for property managers with no consumer anonymous hosting features.

Booking.com.history: Booking.com was founded in 1996 in Amsterdam, Netherlands by Geert-Jan Bruinsma as Bookings.nl. In 2000, it merged with Bookings Online (Bookings.org) and was renamed Booking.com. In July 2005, Priceline Group acquired Booking.com for $133 million, a strategic move that fundamentally transformed the parent company. Booking.com grew into the world's largest online travel agency, now part of Booking Holdings (formerly Priceline Group, renamed in 2018). The platform operates in 40+ languages and lists over 28 million accommodation options worldwide.
Booking.com.summary_abuse: Booking.com has faced significant regulatory scrutiny for dark patterns. The EU investigated and fined the platform for misleading urgency cues (false scarcity messages, countdown timers) and pressure tactics. Dutch consumer authority ACM issued warnings. The platform uses heavy ad tracking across the web and aggressively collects user behavioral data. Despite GDPR compliance measures, Booking.com's commercial dark patterns remain a notable concern for consumer trust.

Booking.com widget.history: The Booking.com widget is an embeddable affiliate and search widget provided by Booking.com (part of Booking Holdings, founded 1996 in Amsterdam) for third-party website operators. It allows affiliate partners to embed hotel search and booking functionality on their own websites. The widget loads Booking.com scripts and tracking pixels onto host pages, enabling revenue sharing for publishers who refer bookings.
Booking.com widget.summary_abuse: The Booking.com widget injects Booking.com's heavy tracking infrastructure onto any host page where it is embedded. This includes advertising trackers, behavioral analytics pixels, and retargeting scripts from Booking.com's extensive ad-tech stack. Publishers embedding this widget expose their visitors to Booking.com's dark pattern tactics (urgency messaging, false scarcity) and heavy cross-site tracking. Moderate-to-high privacy risk for end users of sites using this widget.

Bookingkit.history: Bookingkit was founded in 2014 by Christoph Kruse and Lukas C. C. Hempel in Berlin, Germany. The company received backing from HTGF (High-Tech Gründerfonds) and grew to serve tour and activity operators across Europe. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have occurred since founding.
Bookingkit.summary_abuse: Bookingkit is a European booking management SaaS focused on tours and attractions with no documented abuse signals. The platform requires business registration and is primarily used by legitimate activity operators. No public reports of fraudulent use, phishing, or notable security incidents have been identified.

BookingKoala.history: BookingKoala was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Illinois, USA. It is a specialized SaaS platform targeting home service businesses such as cleaning and landscaping, offering scheduling, quoting, and billing automation. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes since founding.
BookingKoala.summary_abuse: BookingKoala is a niche home-services scheduling SaaS with no documented abuse signals. Its customer-facing booking forms present minimal abuse surface; the platform is used primarily by verified service businesses. No public reports of phishing kit hosting, fake storefront abuse, or notable security incidents have been identified.

Bookitit.history: Bookitit was founded in 2012 and is headquartered in Spain. It provides an online scheduling and appointment booking system primarily for SMBs. No notable acquisitions, rebrands, or ownership changes have been documented.
Bookitit.summary_abuse: Bookitit is a small Spanish appointment scheduling SaaS with no documented abuse signals. The platform is used by local service businesses for client bookings. No reports of fraudulent use, phishing hosting, or security incidents have been identified.

Booklux.history: Booklux OÜ is an Estonian company providing a cloud-based online booking system, with over 10 years of operation. The company is registered in Estonia and primarily serves service businesses in the Baltic region. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Booklux.summary_abuse: Booklux is a small Estonian appointment booking SaaS with no documented abuse signals. The platform is focused on service businesses in the Baltic region and has no known history of fraudulent use or security incidents.

Bookly.history: Bookly is a WordPress appointment booking plugin developed by Nota-info, launched around 2014 on CodeCanyon and later expanded to a direct subscription model. It is used by over 70,000 businesses worldwide. The plugin has a free lite version on WordPress.org and a paid PRO version. No notable ownership changes or controversies have been documented.
Bookly.summary_abuse: Bookly is a widely-used WordPress booking plugin with no notable abuse signals. As a self-hosted plugin, the abuse surface is entirely dependent on how individual site operators configure it. No public reports of Bookly being used for fraudulent bookings or scam operations have been identified.

BookManager.history: Bookmanager was founded in 1986 and has been providing POS and inventory management software for independent booksellers in North America for nearly four decades. Over 400 bookstores currently use the platform.
BookManager.summary_abuse: Bookmanager is a POS and inventory management tool for independent bookstores with no notable abuse signal. It is a backend retail tool with no consumer-facing content hosting or online fraud surface.

BookStack.history: BookStack is an open-source, self-hosted wiki and documentation platform built with PHP and Laravel, first released in 2015. It is MIT licensed and hosted on GitHub/Codeberg. BookStack organizes content in a hierarchical structure of shelves, books, chapters, and pages, providing an intuitive documentation experience. The project supports LDAP, SAML2, and social authentication for enterprise environments. It has become one of the most popular open-source alternatives to Confluence and Notion for self-hosted knowledge management.
BookStack.summary_abuse: No abuse signals. BookStack is a trusted, widely-deployed open-source documentation platform with MIT license. The code is fully transparent and community-audited. Self-hosted deployment provides full data sovereignty. Widely used in enterprise environments as a Confluence alternative. No security incidents or abuse patterns reported.
