Bookster.history: Bookster is a Scottish vacation rental management SaaS based in Edinburgh, UK. The company specialises in tools for holiday rental professionals including booking engines, marketing, and channel management. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Bookster.summary_abuse: Bookster is a small UK-based vacation rental SaaS with no documented abuse signals. The platform serves holiday property operators and has minimal exposure to fraudulent use. No public reports of abuse or security incidents have been identified.

Booksy.history: Booksy was founded in July 2014 by Stefan Batory and Konrad Howard in Warsaw, Poland, with a global-first strategy from inception. Headquartered in Chicago since expansion, the company raised $70M in a Series C round in January 2021 and subsequently raised additional funding totalling over $120M. By 2024 it reported $65.9M in revenue and processed over 260 million appointments annually across five core markets: US, UK, Poland, Spain, and France.
Booksy.summary_abuse: Booksy is a mainstream beauty and wellness booking marketplace with no significant documented abuse signals. The platform connects consumers to salons and barbershops, with verified business profiles. Its consumer-facing marketplace format presents standard risks of fake review manipulation, but no notable phishing, scam hosting, or security incidents have been publicly reported.

Bookteq.history: Bookteq is a UK-based sports and leisure facility booking software platform serving schools, councils, and sports clubs. The company is small and independently operated. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Bookteq.summary_abuse: Bookteq is a niche UK sports facility booking SaaS with no documented abuse signals. The platform is used by institutional venues such as schools and councils, which naturally limits abuse exposure. No public reports of fraudulent use or security incidents have been identified.

BookThatApp.history: BookThatApp (BTA) was launched around 2011 as one of the first dedicated booking apps for Shopify merchants, enabling appointment scheduling, rental management, and class bookings. Originally developed by BTA Commerce Inc., the app was later acquired by Relay Commerce, a company that aggregates multiple Shopify ecosystem tools. It has grown to serve over 6,000 active Shopify stores.
BookThatApp.summary_abuse: BookThatApp has no notable abuse signal. It is a Shopify appointment booking app used by legitimate merchants for scheduling and rental management. As a Shopify ecosystem app, it inherits Shopify's merchant verification requirements. No documented fraud or phishing abuse patterns are associated with this platform.

BookVisit.history: BookVisit is a Swedish hospitality booking platform and business development tool founded around 2000, describing itself as the Nordic leader in hospitality software. No significant ownership changes are documented.
BookVisit.summary_abuse: BookVisit is a Nordic hotel booking engine and hospitality platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves hoteliers and travel operators with no consumer-facing anonymous content hosting features.

Bool TypeScript.history: Bool TypeScript is a lightweight TypeScript framework optimized for the Bun runtime, providing building blocks for high-performance web applications. The project emerged post-2022 as part of the Bun ecosystem. No notable ownership changes or controversies have been documented.
Bool TypeScript.summary_abuse: Bool TypeScript is a small TypeScript/Bun framework library with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signals.

BOOM.history: BOOM is a CMS and website builder product offered by ManaAndisheh, an Iranian cloud services and IT company. ManaAndisheh markets BOOM under its Canvas brand as a platform for building websites and online stores. The company is based in Iran and positions itself as a cloud services provider for the local market.
BOOM.summary_abuse: BOOM is an Iranian-market CMS platform with limited international presence. Iranian-hosted platforms operate outside typical international regulatory frameworks, though no specific documented abuse incidents are associated with this particular product. The platform's primary market is the local Iranian business community.

Boomerang.history: Boomerang was created by Baydin, a company founded in January 2010 by Aye Moah and Alex Moore. The product launched publicly later in 2010 as an email scheduling and reminder add-on for Gmail, then expanded to Outlook. Baydin grew to mid-7-figure revenue with a small team and minimal outside funding, remaining independent. The company also produces The Email Game and Inbox Pause.
Boomerang.summary_abuse: Boomerang is an established email productivity add-on used by nearly 1.5 million businesses with no documented abuse signals. As a Gmail/Outlook extension, it requires OAuth access to users' email, which is a privacy consideration, but the company has no known history of data misuse or public security incidents.

boomtime.history: boomtime is a full-service digital marketing platform based in the US, providing businesses with a managed marketing solution.
boomtime.summary_abuse: boomtime is a B2B managed digital marketing platform with no notable abuse signal.

Boost Commerce.history: Boost Commerce was founded in 2016 by Kamna Karam and Tony Tri Nguyen, initially focused on Shopify product filter and search solutions. The company was acquired by Clearer on July 25, 2023 and rebranded its flagship product as Boost AI Search and Filter. It remains one of the top-rated filter and search apps on the Shopify App Store.
Boost Commerce.summary_abuse: Boost Commerce is a Shopify app provider with no documented abuse signals. The product is a search and filtering layer for Shopify stores and has no mechanism enabling third-party content hosting or fraudulent storefronts. No public reports of abuse or security incidents have been identified.

Boost.ai.history: Boost.ai was founded in 2016 in Stavanger, Norway by Lars Selsas, Henry Iversen, and Hadle Selsas. The company develops enterprise conversational AI and virtual agent technology. In 2023 Nordic Capital announced a partnership and investment. The platform operates 600+ live AI agents and processes over 150 million conversations annually.
Boost.ai.summary_abuse: Boost.ai is an enterprise conversational AI platform sold to large financial institutions and utilities with no documented abuse signals. The product requires enterprise contracts and operates within controlled B2B environments. No public reports of fraudulent use or security incidents have been identified.

Booster Page Speed Optimizer.history: Booster: Page Speed Optimizer is a free Shopify app developed by Booster SEO (formerly Booster Apps), designed to improve page load times through prefetching and lazy loading techniques. The app is available free on the Shopify App Store and has accumulated a large user base among Shopify merchants.
Booster Page Speed Optimizer.summary_abuse: Booster Page Speed Optimizer is a Shopify storefront performance app with no documented abuse signals. It runs on merchant Shopify stores and does not host user-generated content or enable arbitrary file uploads. No public reports of abuse or security incidents have been identified.

Bootbox.js.history: Bootbox.js was created in 2011 by Nick Payne as a small JavaScript library to wrap Bootstrap's modal dialogs into programmatic alert, confirm, and prompt calls. It is maintained at bootboxjs/bootbox on GitHub and has been continuously updated alongside Bootstrap versions. No notable ownership changes or controversies have been documented.
Bootbox.js.summary_abuse: Bootbox.js is a small frontend JavaScript utility library with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signals.

Bootic.history: Bootic is a Chilean e-commerce platform that has operated for over 13 years. It provides hosted online store creation tools for SMBs in Latin America. No notable acquisitions, ownership changes, or controversies have been documented.
Bootic.summary_abuse: Bootic is a small Latin American e-commerce platform builder with limited documented information about abuse posture. As a hosted storefront builder, it could in principle host fake shops, though its primary market is Chile and the platform is small enough that abuse risk is limited. No specific public abuse reports have been identified.

Bootpay.history: Bootpay is a South Korean payment gateway that provides payment processing services for developers and businesses in Korea. Limited public information is available about the company's founding date and history.
Bootpay.summary_abuse: Bootpay is a South Korean payment gateway with no documented abuse signals. As a payments processor operating in a regulated market with KYC requirements, direct fraud facilitation is minimal. No specific public abuse reports have been identified.

Bootstrap.history: Bootstrap was originally named Twitter Blueprint and developed by Mark Otto and Jacob Thornton at Twitter starting in 2010. It was renamed and released as an open-source project on August 19, 2011. Bootstrap 2 added responsive design support in January 2012. Bootstrap 3 in 2013 moved to mobile-first. Bootstrap 4 released in 2018 and Bootstrap 5 in 2021 dropped jQuery dependency. The project transitioned from being Twitter-branded to an independent open-source project maintained by its core team.
Bootstrap.summary_abuse: Bootstrap is a foundational CSS framework with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signals. It is a frontend library downloaded and used by developers in their own projects, with no hosted service or user-generated content capability.

Bootstrap Icons.history: Bootstrap Icons is the official open-source SVG icon library for the Bootstrap framework, designed by Mark Otto and maintained by the Bootstrap core team. It launched around 2020 alongside Bootstrap 5 development. The library contains over 2,000 icons and is distributed via npm and CDN under the MIT license.
Bootstrap Icons.summary_abuse: Bootstrap Icons is an SVG icon library with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signals.

Bootstrap Table.history: Bootstrap Table is an open-source jQuery/Bootstrap plugin for enhanced HTML tables, created by wenzhixin and first published around 2012. It supports Bootstrap, Semantic UI, Bulma, Material Design, and Vue.js. The project is actively maintained on GitHub with a large contributor community. No notable ownership changes or controversies have been documented.
Bootstrap Table.summary_abuse: Bootstrap Table is a frontend table plugin with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signals.

BootstrapCDN.history: BootstrapCDN was originally launched as a dedicated CDN for Bootstrap assets, initially operated via StackPath/MaxCDN. In 2020, jsDelivr became the official CDN partner for Bootstrap, and BootstrapCDN was merged into jsDelivr's infrastructure. The service now distributes Bootstrap, Font Awesome, Bootswatch, and Bootstrap Icons from jsDelivr's global network.
BootstrapCDN.summary_abuse: BootstrapCDN is a static asset delivery CDN for open-source libraries with no documented abuse signals. It serves read-only JavaScript and CSS files and has no user-generated content capability or ability to host malicious pages.

Booxi.history: Booxi was founded in 2013 in Montreal, Canada as a bootstrapped appointment scheduling startup before raising $1.5M in early funding. The company has grown into an enterprise retail scheduling platform used by brands such as LVMH, L'Oreal, Sephora, and Decathlon. It operates in over 80 countries and schedules millions of appointments annually.
Booxi.summary_abuse: Booxi is an enterprise appointment scheduling platform for major retailers with no documented abuse signals. The platform requires enterprise contracts and is used by large, verified retail brands, which effectively limits abuse exposure.

Borderfree.history: Borderfree was founded in 1999 in Israel by Yuval Tal as FiftyOne, a forex conversion service for global retailers. In 2012 it acquired the Borderfree brand from Canada Post and renamed itself. Pitney Bowes acquired Borderfree for approximately $395 million in June 2015. Pitney Bowes later sold Borderfree to Israeli cross-border ecommerce firm Global-e for $100 million.
Borderfree.summary_abuse: Borderfree is an enterprise cross-border ecommerce platform serving major retail brands with no documented abuse signals. The service facilitates legitimate international retail transactions and operates within regulated payments infrastructure. No public reports of fraudulent use or security incidents have been identified.

Borlabs Cookie.history: Borlabs is a German company that has created customized web tools since 2006 and began developing premium WordPress plugins in 2016. Borlabs Cookie is their flagship GDPR and ePrivacy cookie consent management plugin for WordPress. The plugin became widely adopted among European WordPress sites following the enforcement of GDPR in 2018.
Borlabs Cookie.summary_abuse: Borlabs Cookie is a GDPR consent management WordPress plugin with no documented abuse signals. It is a developer tool installed on publishers' own WordPress sites and has no mechanism for hosting user-generated content or facilitating fraudulent use.

Bot9.history: Bot9 is an AI-driven no-code chatbot builder founded in 2020 in Bengaluru, India. It targets small businesses seeking to automate customer support and sales interactions. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Bot9.summary_abuse: Bot9 is a small AI chatbot SaaS with no documented abuse signals specific to this platform. Like other no-code chatbot builders, it could theoretically be used to create deceptive customer service bots, but no public reports of abuse have been identified.

Botble CMS.history: Botble CMS is a PHP content management system built on the Laravel framework, developed by the Botble team in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam since 2015. It is sold primarily on CodeCanyon and offers CRUD generation, modular architecture, and multilingual support. The platform is actively maintained and regularly updated.
Botble CMS.summary_abuse: Botble CMS is a self-hosted Laravel PHP CMS with no documented abuse signals. It is installed by developers on their own servers and has no consumer-facing hosted surface that could be exploited directly.

botBrains.history: botBrains is an AI chatbot SaaS for customer support, targeting businesses seeking automated inquiry handling; no notable founding year or ownership history found in public records.
botBrains.summary_abuse: botBrains is a customer support chatbot platform. It has no consumer-facing hosting surface that would enable phishing or illicit content hosting. No documented abuse patterns have been reported.

Bothive.history: Bothive was founded in 2018 in Ghent, Belgium, providing chatbot-based customer communication tools. The company is a small startup focused on automating online customer interactions for businesses. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Bothive.summary_abuse: Bothive is a small Belgian chatbot SaaS with no documented abuse signals. The platform provides customer communication automation for small businesses in Belgium. No public reports of fraudulent use or security incidents have been identified.

Botmind.history: Botmind was founded in 2017 in Paris, France. It develops hybrid chatbot software for e-commerce customer support automation, using AI to automatically handle frequent customer inquiries. The company reported $2.9M revenue in 2024 with a 17-person team. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Botmind.summary_abuse: Botmind is a small French e-commerce customer support chatbot SaaS with no documented abuse signals. The platform is used by retailers for automating legitimate customer service interactions. No public reports of fraudulent use have been identified.

BotPenguin.history: BotPenguin is an Indian chatbot platform company providing no-code chatbot builder tools for websites, WhatsApp, and social media. The company is based in India and offers a free-forever plan. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
BotPenguin.summary_abuse: BotPenguin is a small Indian no-code chatbot platform with no documented abuse signals. The platform is used by small businesses for customer interaction automation. No specific public reports of fraudulent use have been identified.

Botpress.history: Botpress was founded in 2017 in Montreal, Canada by Sylvain Perron and Justin Watson as one of the first open-source chatbot frameworks. The company hit 1,000 GitHub stars in 2018, launched its Enterprise edition in 2019, raised a $15M Series A in 2021, launched Botpress Cloud in 2023, and raised a $25M Series B in June 2025. The project pivoted toward an LLM-native architecture for agentic AI systems and grew its developer base past 300,000.
Botpress.summary_abuse: Botpress is a widely-used open-source chatbot and AI agent framework with no documented significant abuse signals. The self-hosted open-source version gives developers full control over deployment. The cloud version is subject to standard SaaS AUPs. No notable public abuse incidents have been reported.

Botsify.history: Botsify was founded in 2016 and is headquartered in Karachi, Pakistan. It provides a no-code AI chatbot platform for businesses and reported $269K revenue with 260 customers in 2024. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Botsify.summary_abuse: Botsify is a small Pakistani no-code chatbot platform with no documented abuse signals. The platform enables businesses to build customer service chatbots. No specific public reports of fraudulent use or security incidents have been identified.

Botsonic.history: Botsonic is a no-code AI chatbot builder developed by Writesonic, an AI content generation company founded by Samanyou Garg. Botsonic was launched around 2022 as Writesonic expanded its product suite beyond text generation. It enables businesses to build ChatGPT-powered custom chatbots using their own data. No notable ownership changes outside Writesonic have been documented.
Botsonic.summary_abuse: Botsonic is a no-code AI chatbot builder built on top of large language models. Like other AI chatbot builders, it could theoretically be used to create deceptive or manipulative customer-facing bots. No specific public reports of abuse have been identified for this platform.

Botsplash.history: Botsplash was founded around 2016 and is a US-based conversational AI platform for customer engagement across SMS, chat, and messaging channels. The platform is primarily targeted at financial services and automotive industries. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Botsplash.summary_abuse: Botsplash is a niche conversational AI platform for regulated industries with no documented abuse signals. The platform targets financial services firms and automotive dealers, which operate in tightly regulated environments. No public reports of fraudulent use have been identified.

BotStar.history: BotStar is a chatbot platform offering drag-and-drop bot building for Facebook Messenger and web chat. The platform is popular in Vietnam and Southeast Asia. No notable acquisitions or major ownership changes have been documented.
BotStar.summary_abuse: BotStar is a small chatbot builder with no documented abuse signals. The platform enables businesses to create customer service bots. No specific public reports of fraudulent use or security incidents have been identified.

Bottle.history: Bottle is a fast, simple, and lightweight Python WSGI micro web framework created by Marcel Hellkamp, first released in July 2009 after about three days of development. It was conceived as a minimal single-file framework with no dependencies outside the Python standard library. The project remains maintained on GitHub under the MIT license.
Bottle.summary_abuse: Bottle is a foundational Python micro-framework with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signals.

Bottle360.history: Bottle360 is a US-based all-in-one DTC winery software platform serving wineries with point-of-sale, wine club management, and e-commerce capabilities. Founded by Katherine Adams (CEO) and Tim Murry (Co-Founder & VP), the company is known for maintaining stable pricing and has a 5-star customer review track record.
Bottle360.summary_abuse: Bottle360 is a niche winery management SaaS with no documented abuse signals. The platform serves verified winery businesses and has no known history of fraudulent use or security incidents.

BotUp.history: BotUp is a chatbot builder product from 500apps, a suite of 50+ SaaS applications. The platform enables building chatbots for Facebook, WhatsApp, and Slack without coding. It is part of the 500apps umbrella and priced as part of the all-apps flat subscription model.
BotUp.summary_abuse: BotUp is a no-code chatbot builder bundled into the 500apps SaaS suite with no documented abuse signals. The platform is used for basic customer interaction automation. No specific public reports of fraudulent use have been identified.

Boulevard.history: Boulevard was founded in 2016 by Sean Stavropoulos and Matt Danna in Los Angeles. The company raised $11M Series A in November 2019, $27M Series B in November 2020, $70M Series C, and $80M Series D in July 2025, valuing the company at approximately $800M. Boulevard provides SaaS-based booking, scheduling, and payment software for salons, spas, and wellness businesses.
Boulevard.summary_abuse: Boulevard is a well-funded salon and spa management SaaS with no documented abuse signals. The platform processes payments and bookings for beauty businesses and operates under standard financial compliance. No public reports of fraudulent use or security incidents have been identified.

Bounce Commerce.history: Bounce Commerce GmbH was founded in 2021 in Geldern, Germany. The company provides AI-based bounce management and conversion optimization solutions for e-commerce, working with over 320 advertisers worldwide. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Bounce Commerce.summary_abuse: Bounce Commerce is a small German e-commerce conversion optimization tool with no documented abuse signals. The platform focuses on reducing bounce rates for online retailers. No public reports of fraudulent use have been identified.

Boutiq.history: Boutiq (by Caazam Inc) is a Shopify app providing AI-powered video clienteling for e-commerce stores, enabling 1:1 video shopping sessions between merchants and customers. It targets fashion and lifestyle brands seeking a personal shopping experience online. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Boutiq.summary_abuse: Boutiq is a niche video shopping Shopify app with no documented abuse signals. The platform facilitates live video interactions between merchants and shoppers. No public reports of fraudulent use have been identified.

Boutir.history: Boutir was launched in 2015 in Hong Kong as a simple mobile-first ecommerce platform targeting small businesses in Hong Kong and Southeast Asia. The platform focuses on mobile store creation and social commerce. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Boutir.summary_abuse: Boutir is a Hong Kong-based ecommerce store builder with a free tier allowing users to host online shops. As a hosted storefront builder, it could in principle be used to create fraudulent storefronts, though its primary market is small legitimate retailers in Hong Kong. No specific public abuse reports have been identified.

BowNow.history: BowNow was established in January 2021 as a Japanese marketing automation SaaS product. It holds a leading market share among Japanese-origin marketing automation tools. The platform is designed specifically for the Japanese market and offers a free tier. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
BowNow.summary_abuse: BowNow is a Japanese marketing automation tool with no documented abuse signals. The platform targets B2B lead management and email marketing for Japanese businesses. No public reports of abuse or security incidents have been identified.

bowser.history: bowser is a browser/platform/engine detection JavaScript library created around 2012. It is widely used and maintained independently with no significant ownership events.
bowser.summary_abuse: bowser is a JavaScript browser detection library with no notable abuse signal. It is a pure client-side utility with no server-side or content-hosting surface.

Bowtie.history: Bowtie Life Insurance Company was founded in 2018 and in December 2018 became the first company to be granted a virtual insurance licence by the Hong Kong Insurance Authority. The company raised $30M at launch, attracting investment from Sun Life Hong Kong in a later Series C funding round in 2025. Bowtie focuses on direct-to-consumer online health and life insurance in Hong Kong.
Bowtie.summary_abuse: Bowtie is a licensed virtual insurer in Hong Kong operating under Insurance Authority regulation. As a regulated financial services provider, it has strong KYC and anti-fraud requirements. No documented abuse signals or security incidents have been identified.

Boxmode.history: Boxmode was founded in 2020 by Max Yarchevsky, a software developer with approximately 20 years of experience. The platform provides a drag-and-drop website builder with a free tier offering custom domains. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Boxmode.summary_abuse: Boxmode is a small website builder offering free hosting under its own subdomains. As a free website hosting platform, it could be used to create phishing pages or fraudulent websites, similar to other free website builders in the market. No specific documented abuse incidents have been identified for this platform.

Boxtal.history: Boxtal was founded in 2009 in Paris as EnvoiMoinsCher.com (Send for Less), a multi-carrier shipping aggregator for French SMBs and e-commerce businesses. The company rebranded to Boxtal in 2016 to better reflect its international shipping capabilities. It offers integrations with WooCommerce, PrestaShop, and other e-commerce platforms via a WordPress plugin.
Boxtal.summary_abuse: Boxtal is a French shipping aggregator with no documented abuse signals. The platform processes parcel shipments for legitimate businesses and requires business registration. No public reports of fraudulent use or security incidents have been identified.

Bpost.history: Bpost traces its origins to 1830 when Belgium established its national postal service after independence. The company underwent several transformations over two centuries and was listed on Euronext Brussels in a 2013 IPO valued near €2.9 billion. It has since expanded from a domestic mail carrier to an international parcel logistics group operating in Europe, North America, and Asia.
Bpost.summary_abuse: Bpost is the Belgian national postal operator and publicly traded logistics company. As a regulated postal service, it operates under strict national and EU regulations. Parcel delivery services are occasionally implicated in shipment of illicit goods by third parties, but this is characteristic of all postal operators rather than a platform-specific abuse signal.

Brafton.history: Brafton was founded in 2008 by William Bracken and Tom Agnew in Boston, Massachusetts. The company operates as a content marketing agency offering content creation, SEO, and digital marketing services. It ranked 61st in Inc. 500 fastest-growing private companies. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Brafton.summary_abuse: Brafton is a content marketing agency with no documented abuse signals. The company provides content creation and SEO services to businesses. No public reports of fraudulent use or security incidents have been identified.

Brainlead.history: Brainlead is an Italian marketing automation software product. Limited public information is available about its founding date or company history.
Brainlead.summary_abuse: Brainlead is a small Italian marketing automation tool with no documented abuse signals. The platform is used by businesses for lead management and email automation. No public reports of fraudulent use have been identified.

BrainSINS.history: BrainSINS was founded in 2010 in Madrid, Spain by Jose Carlos Cortizo Perez and Frankie Carrero. The company provides e-commerce personalization and analytics solutions. In 2012 the company closed a €400K funding round. No notable acquisitions or major ownership changes have been documented.
BrainSINS.summary_abuse: BrainSINS is a niche e-commerce personalization SaaS with no documented abuse signals. The platform operates as a behavioral analytics and recommendation engine for online retailers. No public reports of fraudulent use have been identified.

Braintree.history: Braintree was founded in 2007 by Bryan Johnson in Chicago as a payment gateway for e-commerce. In 2012 it acquired Venmo for $26.2M. PayPal (then under eBay) acquired Braintree in September 2013 for $800 million in cash. Post-acquisition the platform grew by over 25x in transaction volume by 2016. Braintree is now PayPal's developer-focused payment infrastructure brand.
Braintree.summary_abuse: Braintree is a major payment gateway owned by PayPal with strong KYC, PCI-DSS compliance, and fraud detection. While payment processors are occasionally exploited by fraudulent merchants, Braintree has robust risk and fraud controls and operates under extensive regulatory oversight. Occasional fraud-related incidents with individual merchants are industry-standard, not indicative of platform-level weakness.

Branch.history: Branch Metrics was founded on April 15, 2014 by Alex Austin, Mike Molinet, Mada Seghete, and Dmitri Gaskin. The company raised $15M Series A, achieved unicorn status with $1B+ valuation in 2018, and raised $300M in a round valuing it at $4 billion, with total funding of approximately $667M from NEA, Founders Fund, and others. Branch provides mobile deep linking and attribution infrastructure.
Branch.summary_abuse: Branch is a major mobile attribution and deep linking platform. Deep linking technology has been occasionally observed in malicious mobile campaigns to redirect users to fraudulent destinations, though this is typically misuse by third parties rather than a Branch-specific issue. Branch has standard AUPs and security controls appropriate for a well-funded company at this scale.

BrandBuilderAI.history: BrandBuilderAI is a marketing automation and lead generation system. Limited public information is available about its founding date or detailed history.
BrandBuilderAI.summary_abuse: BrandBuilderAI is a small marketing automation SaaS with no documented abuse signals. The platform is used by small businesses for automated marketing and lead generation. No public reports of fraudulent use have been identified.

Brandfolder.history: Brandfolder was founded in 2012 by Brian Parks, Paul Arterburn, and Ryan Urabe in Denver, Colorado as a digital asset management platform. The company raised $9.89 million in funding from investors including Techstars and Telescope Partners. Brandfolder grew to serve clients including Zoom, TripAdvisor, and Lyft, reaching approximately $24 million in revenue and 4,000 customers by 2020. In August 2020, Smartsheet acquired Brandfolder for approximately $155 million. Under Smartsheet ownership, Brandfolder continues to operate as a standalone DAM product while integrating with the broader Smartsheet work management ecosystem.
Brandfolder.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals found for Brandfolder. It is a reputable DAM platform owned by publicly traded Smartsheet. No reports of data misuse, deceptive practices, or security incidents have been found in public sources.

Brandquiz.history: Brandquiz was launched in January 2017 in Vienna, Austria by Florian Burmann, Stefan Rasch, and Vlad Gozman as an interactive quiz and contest builder for brands. The platform allowed companies to create branded quizzes for lead generation and customer engagement. The company subsequently rebranded as involve.me, expanding beyond quizzes to a broader interactive content and funnel builder platform. The brandquiz.io domain and brand identity continue to exist alongside the involve.me rebrand, serving users seeking quiz-specific tooling.
Brandquiz.summary_abuse: No significant abuse signals found for Brandquiz or its parent involve.me. The platform is a B2B marketing tool with no public reports of data misuse or deceptive practices. As a lead generation tool it handles user data, so privacy practices should be reviewed before deployment.

Braze.history: Braze was founded in 2011 as Appboy by Bill Magnuson, Jon Hyman, and Mark Ghermezian in New York. The company rebranded from Appboy to Braze in 2017. Braze IPO'd on Nasdaq on November 17, 2021, raising $520 million at a market valuation of approximately $5.9 billion. It is a major publicly-traded customer engagement platform used by Fortune 500 companies for multi-channel marketing automation.
Braze.summary_abuse: Braze is a publicly-traded enterprise marketing automation platform with no specific abuse signals beyond typical mass-email marketing risks. As an email and push notification infrastructure, Braze-enabled campaigns could theoretically be exploited for spam or phishing, but Braze operates strict sender policies and compliance controls. No documented cases of deliberate facilitation of abusive campaigns have been reported.

Bread.history: Bread was founded in 2014 as a buy now, pay later (BNPL) platform for e-commerce. In October 2020, Alliance Data acquired Bread for $450 million. Alliance Data later rebranded as Bread Financial in March 2022, taking the name from its BNPL unit. Bread now operates as the merchant-facing BNPL platform of Bread Financial Holdings.
Bread.summary_abuse: Bread is a BNPL platform owned by the publicly-traded Bread Financial. As a regulated financial product operating under federal consumer credit laws, it is subject to strong compliance and KYC requirements. No documented platform-specific abuse signals have been identified.

Bread & Butter.history: Bread & Butter is a first-party data CDP (Customer Data Platform) for lead generation and identity resolution. Limited public information is available about its founding date or company history.
Bread & Butter.summary_abuse: Bread & Butter is a data CDP platform used for lead generation. As a data platform handling first-party consumer data, it is subject to privacy regulations. No specific public abuse reports have been identified.

Breadcrumb NavXT.history: Breadcrumb NavXT is the successor to the earlier Breadcrumb Navigation XT WordPress plugin, developed by mtekk. The plugin generates breadcrumb navigation trails for WordPress sites and has been actively maintained for many years as one of the longest-standing breadcrumb plugins in the WordPress ecosystem. No notable ownership changes have occurred.
Breadcrumb NavXT.summary_abuse: Breadcrumb NavXT is a WordPress navigation plugin with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signals.

Breakdance.history: Breakdance is a WordPress page builder launched in September 2022 by Soflyy, a company with over 15 years of WordPress software experience known for the WP All Import plugin. Breakdance launched with a generous free tier and attracted over 18,000 free users quickly. It offers locked-in pricing guarantees on renewal.
Breakdance.summary_abuse: Breakdance is a self-hosted WordPress page builder with no consumer-facing service surface and no documented abuse signals.

Breeze.history: Breeze is an open-source frontend framework for Magento/Adobe Commerce designed to replace Luma as a lightweight, performance-optimized storefront theme. The project is maintained on GitHub and emerged around 2021 as an alternative to Adobe's default frontend. No notable ownership changes have been documented.
Breeze.summary_abuse: Breeze is an open-source Magento frontend framework with no consumer-facing hosted service and no documented abuse signals.

Breezy HR.history: Breezy HR was founded in 2014 as an applicant tracking and recruiting platform headquartered in the United States. The company built a cloud-based ATS focused on simplifying hiring workflows for small and mid-sized businesses, offering features like drag-and-drop candidate pipelines and automated job postings to major boards. It was later acquired by Learning Technologies Group (LTG), a UK-based workforce learning and talent management conglomerate. Under LTG ownership, Breezy HR continues to operate as a standalone product targeting SMBs seeking streamlined recruitment tooling.
Breezy HR.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals found for Breezy HR. It is a reputable ATS product used widely by SMBs. No reports of data misuse, deceptive practices, or security breaches were found in public sources.

Breinify.history: Breinify was founded in 2016 in San Francisco, California by Diane Keng and Philipp Meisen. The company provides an AI-powered personalization engine designed for consumer brands, using machine learning to deliver real-time individualized recommendations and experiences across digital channels. It positioned itself as an alternative to expensive enterprise personalization suites, targeting mid-market and enterprise retailers. The startup raised venture funding and developed a temporal data platform enabling brands to adapt messaging and offers based on user behavior patterns.
Breinify.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals found for Breinify. The platform is a B2B AI personalization engine with no public reports of data misuse or security incidents. Privacy practices appear standard for a marketing technology vendor.

Brevo.history: Brevo was founded in 2012 in Paris, France by Armand Thiberge under the name Sendinblue. The platform initially focused on transactional and marketing email for SMBs. In October 2020 it raised $160 million in a Series B led by Bridgepoint, Bpifrance, and BlackRock, one of the largest fundraises for a French tech company at the time. In May 2023, Sendinblue rebranded to Brevo to signal its evolution from email marketing into a full CRM and marketing automation suite. By 2023 Brevo had surpassed $100 million ARR while remaining profitable, and in 2025 it became a unicorn after a 500 million euro equity round, valuing it over $1 billion. It serves over 600,000 customers across 180 countries.
Brevo.summary_abuse: Brevo has a generally positive trust profile. There are occasional reports of spam originating from shared IP pools, common for email service providers. GDPR compliance is a stated priority given its French headquarters. No major data breaches or deceptive practices have been widely reported.

Bricks.history: Bricks is a visual WordPress site builder created by Thomas Ehrig and publicly launched in March 2021. The idea began forming at the end of 2017, with the company formally established in 2018 in Paphos, Cyprus. Bricks differentiates from other WordPress builders by generating clean semantic HTML and offering full code editing alongside its visual interface. It quickly gained a strong following among developers and designers seeking a performant alternative to Elementor or Divi, and has been regularly updated with new features including query loops, dynamic data, and custom CSS capabilities.
Bricks.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Bricks Builder. It is a self-hosted WordPress plugin with a clean reputation in the WordPress developer community. As a local software installation, it does not collect user data by default.

Bricksite.history: Bricksite was founded in 2005 and is headquartered in Randers (also cited as Lystrup), Denmark, operating as Bricksite ApS. The company offers a simple WYSIWYG website builder allowing users to create and manage websites with custom domains and email. The platform targets individuals and small businesses wanting an easy entry point for online presence. Bricksite operates with a very small team of approximately four employees split between Denmark and Malaysia, and offers three pricing tiers including a no-duration-limit free plan.
Bricksite.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals found for Bricksite. It is a small Danish website builder with no reports of data misuse or security incidents. Given its small team size and longevity since 2005, it appears to be a stable niche product.

BridgerPay.history: BridgerPay was founded in 2018 or 2019 by Ran Cohen and is headquartered in Limassol, Cyprus. The company describes itself as a payment operations platform with a drag-and-drop interface for connecting multiple payment methods and acquirers through a single API. It targets online businesses seeking to optimize payment routing, reduce processing fees, and expand into new markets without changing their underlying business logic. The platform has 51-200 employees and positions itself as a payment orchestration layer rather than a direct processor.
BridgerPay.summary_abuse: BridgerPay operates in the payment orchestration space from Cyprus, which carries some geographic risk. No specific abuse incidents were found, but payment intermediaries carry inherent risk and should be evaluated for PCI DSS compliance before use. The platform appears focused on legitimate B2B use cases.

Bridgetown.history: Bridgetown is an open-source Ruby-powered static site generator and fullstack framework maintained by Jared White of the Portland-based web studio Whitefusion. It was created as a fork and modern reimagining of Jekyll, aiming to improve on Jekyll's aging architecture with faster build times, better Webpack integration, and Ruby component support. The project was announced around 2020 and is hosted on GitHub, funded through GitHub Sponsors. Bridgetown has since evolved beyond static generation into a fullstack web framework built on Roda and esbuild, embracing an HTML-first approach.
Bridgetown.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for Bridgetown. It is an MIT-licensed open-source project with no data collection or commercial tracking. As a static site generator, it poses no privacy risks to end users by design.

Brightcove.history: Brightcove was founded in 2004 by Jeremy Allaire and Bob Mason in Boston, Massachusetts. The company pioneered cloud-based video hosting and streaming infrastructure for media companies and enterprises. It raised $103 million in venture funding from General Catalyst and Accel Partners before going public in 2012 on the NASDAQ. Brightcove became a leading enterprise online video platform, serving global media companies and brands. In February 2025, Italian tech firm Bending Spoons acquired Brightcove for $233 million, taking it private again.
Brightcove.summary_abuse: No major abuse signals for Brightcove. It is a well-established enterprise video platform with 20+ years of operation and a strong track record. The acquisition by Bending Spoons in 2025 may introduce some uncertainty around long-term product direction.

BrightEdge.history: BrightEdge was founded in 2007 by Jim Yu and Lemuel Park in Foster City, California. The company built an enterprise SEO platform that connects organic search performance to business revenue metrics, helping large brands manage content at scale. BrightEdge raised $63.4 million in funding from investors including Battery Ventures, Insight Partners, and Intel Capital. By 2024 it had grown to approximately $120 million in annual revenue with around 500 employees and 1,500 direct customers. The platform is used by 57% of the Fortune 100 and claims to be the most widely adopted enterprise SEO platform globally.
BrightEdge.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for BrightEdge. It is a well-established enterprise SEO platform with a strong reputation among large brands. No reports of data misuse, deceptive practices, or security incidents were found in public sources.

BrightInfo.history: BrightInfo was founded in 2012 in Tel Aviv, Israel by Boaz Grinvald. The company built a real-time content personalization engine that tracks individual buyer journeys and delivers relevant content recommendations across web, mobile, social, and email touchpoints. It integrates with marketing automation platforms like HubSpot and Marketo. Notable customers included Cisco and Attunity. The company appears to have reduced activity in recent years with limited public updates.
BrightInfo.summary_abuse: BrightInfo is a content personalization tracker that monitors visitor behavior across websites. As a behavioral tracking technology, it collects significant user data and should be evaluated against GDPR and privacy requirements before deployment. No major data breach incidents found.

BrightLocal.history: BrightLocal was founded in 2008-2009 by Myles Anderson and Ed Eliot in Brighton, United Kingdom. The company initially operated as a local SEO agency before pivoting to software in 2010-2012, launching tools for local rank tracking, citation management, and audits. By 2014 it had reached $1 million in revenue. The platform grew significantly, reaching over $32 million in revenue and 168 employees by 2024. BrightLocal serves over 15,000 small businesses and marketing agencies and has built a reputation as a leading local SEO toolset with 16 years of proprietary local search data.
BrightLocal.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for BrightLocal. It is an established UK-based local SEO platform with a strong market reputation. No reports of data misuse or security incidents. GDPR compliance is expected given its UK base.

Brightspot.history: Brightspot was founded in 2008 as Perfect Sense Digital by David Gang in Reston, Virginia. The company built an enterprise CMS platform tailored for media publishers and digital experience teams, with a focus on enabling fast content publishing at scale. It raised $22 million from Carrick Capital Partners. In August 2020, the company rebranded from Perfect Sense to Brightspot to better reflect its expanded product vision. Notable customers include FedEx, Hallmark, and Wayfair.
Brightspot.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Brightspot. It is an established enterprise CMS with a reputable client roster. No reports of data misuse, deceptive practices, or security incidents were found.

Brilliant Web-to-Lead.history: Brilliant Web-to-Lead for Salesforce is a WordPress plugin developed by BrilliantPlugins that creates an integration between WordPress websites and Salesforce CRM. The plugin allows website visitors to submit contact forms that automatically create leads or cases in Salesforce, eliminating manual data entry. It supports configurable form fields, UTM tracking integration, dynamic field population, and importing Web-to-Lead forms directly from Salesforce. The plugin is freely available on WordPress.org and maintained on GitHub.
Brilliant Web-to-Lead.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for Brilliant Web-to-Lead. It is a straightforward open-source WordPress plugin for Salesforce integration with no tracking or data collection beyond the form submissions it is explicitly designed to relay to Salesforce.

Brimble.history: Brimble was founded in 2021 in Delaware, United States by Abraham Ugbeshe and David Muritala. The platform is a PaaS (Platform as a Service) designed to simplify deployment of web applications, allowing developers to build, launch, and scale apps without managing servers or piecing together multiple providers. Brimble offers full-stack app deployment, managed databases (MySQL, Postgres, Redis), and AI model integrations through a single platform. The company entered public beta in June 2024 and operates with a small team of 2-10 employees.
Brimble.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for Brimble. It is a small early-stage cloud hosting startup with no public reports of incidents. As an early-stage company with a very small team, there is inherent vendor stability risk.

Bringie.history: Bringie is a conversion-boosting platform that provides a suite of widgets for websites including live sales notifications, visitor counters, countdown timers, pop-ups, and special offer modules. The platform markets itself as a 24-in-1 conversion tool to help e-commerce sites increase conversions. Limited public information is available about the company's founding date, location, or team behind the product.
Bringie.summary_abuse: Bringie provides social proof and urgency marketing widgets which can be used to create misleading impression of demand or artificial scarcity. Such tools carry moderate dark pattern risk depending on how they are configured. The company has very limited public information available.

Brink Commerce.history: Brink Commerce was founded in 2020 in Stockholm, Sweden by Kristian Tysander and Patrik Antonsson, who previously worked as CTO at Daniel Wellington and in cloud infrastructure at H&M. The founders experienced severe capacity issues during high-traffic product launches and built Brink as a serverless, headless eCommerce API capable of handling over 6,500 orders per second. Backed by Wellstreet and investor Filip Tysander, the platform is a certified MACH Alliance member, supporting composable commerce architectures for enterprise brands.
Brink Commerce.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for Brink Commerce. It is a legitimate enterprise eCommerce infrastructure company with a clean track record. Founded by experienced e-commerce engineers from well-known Scandinavian brands.

Briqpay.history: Briqpay was founded in 2020 in Bromma, Sweden by Bjorn Widerstrom, Mats Andersson, and Sara Johansson, all of whom previously worked at Klarna. The company provides a B2B checkout platform for e-commerce, offering flexible payment methods, instant credit lookups, and customizable checkout flows for business buyers. Briqpay raised a total of approximately $2.35 million over two funding rounds, including a 2 million euro seed round. The platform focuses on simplifying B2B online payments which has traditionally lagged consumer checkout experiences.
Briqpay.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for Briqpay. It is a legitimate Swedish B2B payment startup founded by fintech veterans. As a payment processor it handles financial transactions, which require proper PCI compliance, but no misconduct was found.

Brivity.history: Brivity was founded in 2012 in Bellingham, Washington by real estate agent and entrepreneur Ben Kinney. Kinney built the platform to automate lead generation and follow-up for real estate agents, consolidating transaction management, CRM, and marketing in one system. Brivity became the flagship software product of the Ben Kinney Companies portfolio, which also includes Automabots, Redman Tech, and Gabbi.ai. The platform serves real estate agents and brokerages across the United States with 51-200 employees.
Brivity.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Brivity. It is a US-based real estate CRM with a straightforward product offering and no public reports of data misuse or security incidents.

Brizy.history: Brizy is a WordPress page builder plugin developed and maintained by ThemeFuse, a premium WordPress theme shop founded in 2009 and based in London, England. Brizy was launched as a plugin in 2018 and quickly attracted attention for its clean drag-and-drop interface and focus on ease of use for non-technical users. ThemeFuse has over 11 years of WordPress experience and claims 800,000+ customers and nearly 4 million downloads across its themes and plugins. Brizy has grown to over 180,000 customers and almost 970,000 downloads, and also has a cloud-based SaaS variant called Brizy Cloud.
Brizy.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Brizy. It is a GPL-licensed WordPress plugin with a free version on WordPress.org and a well-established parent company in ThemeFuse. No reports of security issues or data misuse.

Brizy Cloud.history: Brizy Cloud is the hosted SaaS variant of the Brizy page builder, developed by ThemeFuse, a London-based WordPress theme and plugin company founded in 2009. While the WordPress plugin version launched in 2018, Brizy Cloud was introduced as an all-in-one hosted website builder with integrated hosting, eliminating the need for a separate WordPress installation. It offers plans for individuals, agencies, and white-label usage, and added AI-driven design capabilities. The platform targets non-technical users and agencies building sites for clients.
Brizy Cloud.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Brizy Cloud. It is a hosted website builder product from ThemeFuse, a company with over a decade of presence in the WordPress ecosystem. The white-label option means third-party resellers may use the underlying platform.

Brizy WordPress.history: Brizy WordPress is the self-hosted WordPress plugin variant of the Brizy page builder, created by ThemeFuse, a company with roots in WordPress theme development since 2009. The plugin was released in 2018 and provides a drag-and-drop visual editing experience directly on the WordPress frontend. It is available free on WordPress.org with optional paid pro extensions. ThemeFuse has 800,000+ customers across themes and plugins and Brizy WordPress has accumulated nearly 970,000 downloads, making it one of the more popular page builder plugins.
Brizy WordPress.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for Brizy WordPress. The plugin is GPL-licensed, freely available on WordPress.org, and maintained by an established WordPress vendor. No security incidents or data collection concerns were found.

Broadstreet.history: Broadstreet was founded in 2012 by Kenny Katzgrau and John Crepezzi and is based in Montclair, New Jersey. The company built an ad manager specifically designed for local news and magazine publishers who sell directly to local advertisers. In 2016, Broadstreet launched its XPRESS ad server product. The platform delivers several billion ad impressions per month to an audience of over 50 million across hundreds of local news and magazine publishing groups. Broadstreet focuses on helping niche media organizations compete in an increasingly challenging ad market.
Broadstreet.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Broadstreet. It is a niche ad server for local publishers, not a third-party ad network. Publishers use it to serve ads they have directly sold, which reduces programmatic tracking concerns somewhat. Standard advertising tracking applies.

Bronto.history: Bronto Software was founded in 2002 by Joe Colopy and Chaz Felix in Durham, North Carolina. The company provided marketing automation and email marketing software to mid-market and enterprise customers, growing to over 300 employees and $50 million in revenue with offices in Durham, New York, Los Angeles, London, and Sydney. In April 2015, NetSuite acquired Bronto for $200 million. Oracle subsequently acquired NetSuite in October 2016 for $9.2 billion, bringing Bronto into Oracle's portfolio. In March 2021, Oracle NetSuite announced Bronto's end-of-life, with all services discontinued on May 31, 2022.
Bronto.summary_abuse: Bronto Software had no known abuse incidents during its operation. The platform was shut down by Oracle in May 2022 after its acquisition through the NetSuite purchase. Any websites still showing Bronto scripts likely have outdated code from before the shutdown.

Brownie.history: Brownie Suite is a Spanish omni-channel ERP, CMS, and ecommerce platform built by NSS Factory for managing retail operations both online and in-store. No public founding year or ownership changes are documented.
Brownie.summary_abuse: Brownie Suite is a niche retail ERP and ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves merchants for inventory and store management with no consumer-facing anonymous hosting features.

Browsee.history: Browsee is a session recording and analytics platform providing heatmaps, user session replays, funnel analysis, and UX feedback tools for websites. The platform helps businesses understand how visitors interact with their websites. Limited public company information is available regarding exact founding date or team details. The service is positioned as an alternative to more expensive tools like Fullstory.
Browsee.summary_abuse: Browsee records all visitor sessions and keystrokes by definition, making it a high-privacy-impact tool. Session replay technologies have faced regulatory scrutiny under GDPR and CCPA for potentially capturing sensitive user input. Organizations should ensure proper user consent before deploying Browsee.

Browser-Update.org.history: Browser-Update.org is an open-source project and free service that notifies website visitors with outdated browsers to update. It works by embedding a small JavaScript snippet that displays an unobtrusive notification bar when a visitor is using a browser below a configured minimum version. The project is MIT-licensed and maintains a GitHub repository. The script explicitly does not track users or collect personal information. It was contributed to by Mendix and is maintained by the browser-update community on GitHub.
Browser-Update.org.summary_abuse: Browser-Update.org is a privacy-respecting open-source project. The script explicitly does not track users or collect personal information, making it one of the safest third-party scripts available. No abuse signals found.

BrowserCMS.history: BrowserCMS is an open-source Ruby on Rails web content management system developed by BrowserMedia and released under the LGPL license. The project copyright dates to 1998-2014 per its source code. It was designed as a general-purpose CMS that could be used standalone or integrated into existing Rails applications. The project has not received updates in several years and is considered abandoned, with the GitHub repository under browsermedia/browsercms no longer actively maintained.
BrowserCMS.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for BrowserCMS. It is an abandoned open-source Rails CMS with no data collection or tracking. The main concern with using abandoned software is the lack of security patches for newly discovered vulnerabilities.

BRT.history: Bartolini was founded in 1928 in Bologna, Italy, as a parcel courier service. It grew to become one of Italy's largest courier companies before joining the DPDgroup network in 2016 and becoming 85% owned by La Poste/Geopost in August 2019. It was subsequently rebranded as BRT (Bartolini) and operates as part of Geopost's European delivery network.
BRT.summary_abuse: BRT is a legitimate Italian courier company detected via its parcel tracking and shipping integration widgets on ecommerce sites. Like other major courier brands, its name is occasionally used in package-delivery phishing SMS and email scams targeting Italian consumers, where fraudsters impersonate BRT to steal payment details. BRT itself is not a platform for hosting abusive content; the abuse is impersonation of its brand rather than misuse of its systems.

Brushd.history: Brushd is a cloud-based portfolio builder for creative professionals, founded in 2009 and based in Pleasant Garden, North Carolina. The platform helps artists, photographers, and other creatives build professional online portfolios without requiring technical expertise. Features include image and video galleries, blog support, custom domains, and Etsy shop integration. The company is small and self-funded, operating since 2009 with a focus on serving the creative community.
Brushd.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Brushd. It is a small portfolio hosting platform for creatives with no reports of data misuse. As a small US-based hosting provider, standard privacy considerations apply.

BRYNK.history: BRYNK is an all-in-one membership, donor, event, and fundraising management platform. Limited public history available.
BRYNK.summary_abuse: BRYNK has no notable abuse signal. It is a fundraising and membership management platform with no consumer-facing content hosting that would attract fraudsters.

Bsale.history: Bsale was founded in 2012 in Santiago, Chile by siblings Gustavo and Elvira Montero, who designed a cloud-based sales system to help Chilean small and medium-sized businesses manage their operations. The platform provides point-of-sale, inventory management, electronic receipts and invoices, and multi-channel e-commerce capabilities. Bsale has grown to serve over 10,000 entrepreneurs with 113,000 connected users across Chile, Peru, and Mexico, with 100-200 employees.
Bsale.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Bsale. It is a legitimate Latin American SMB software provider with a decade of operation. No reports of data misuse or security incidents found.

bSecure.history: bSecure was founded in 2020 by Arshad Ali in Karachi, Pakistan, as a one-click checkout and payment gateway solution for Pakistani e-commerce. It targets WooCommerce, Magento, and Shopify merchants operating in Pakistan.
bSecure.summary_abuse: bSecure is a regional payment gateway focused on Pakistan with no documented notable abuse cases. As a payments processor it applies merchant vetting, but public information on its anti-fraud posture is limited.

BSmart.history: BSmart is an Israeli e-commerce platform programmed by Microline. It serves the Israeli market. Limited public history available.
BSmart.summary_abuse: BSmart has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche Israeli e-commerce platform with no documented fraud patterns.

Bsport.history: Bsport was founded in 2018 in Paris, France by Zakaria Mansour and Gabriel Pichot. The company provides a management platform for boutique fitness studios and wellness businesses, offering class booking, scheduling, billing, marketing automation, and mobile apps. In December 2024, bsport raised a Series B of 30 million euros, becoming Europe's leading platform in the wellness studio segment with over 1,500 partners across 30 countries and more than 600,000 active community members. The company has approximately 252 employees as of early 2026.
Bsport.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for bsport. It is a legitimate French SaaS company with significant VC backing and a growing customer base in the boutique fitness market. GDPR compliance is expected given its Paris headquarters.

BTOLEAD.history: BTOLEAD is an Italian B2B lead generation software platform for identifying and connecting with prospective customers. Limited public history available.
BTOLEAD.summary_abuse: BTOLEAD is a B2B lead generation platform. Such tools scrape and aggregate business contact data, which can raise data privacy concerns and be used for cold outreach campaigns that may constitute spam under GDPR. No specific documented abuse incidents.

Bubble.history: Bubble was founded in 2012 in New York City by Emmanuel Straschnov and Josh Haas. For nearly seven years the founders bootstrapped the company, working from coffee shops and coworking spaces. Bubble had its first paying customer in December 2012. In 2019 the company raised a $6.5 million seed round after years of organic growth. In July 2021 it raised a $100 million Series A led by Insight Partners, bringing total funding to $106.3 million. The no-code platform enables users to build full-stack web and mobile applications without writing code. By August 2025, over 4.7 million applications had been built on Bubble, and users had raised over $15 billion in funding collectively.
Bubble.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Bubble. It is a well-funded, reputable no-code platform with SOC 2 Type 2 compliance. As apps built on Bubble can be published externally, any third-party app built on Bubble inherits the platform's security posture but may have independent abuse risks.

Budbee.history: Budbee was founded in 2015 in Stockholm, Sweden by Fredrik Hamilton and Axel Warg. The company specializes in last-mile delivery for e-commerce, using proprietary algorithms and machine learning to optimize delivery routes and provide real-time tracking with precise one-hour delivery windows. Budbee expanded across Northern Europe including Denmark, Norway, Finland, Netherlands, and Belgium. In 2022, Budbee merged with Instabox to form Instabee, which also operates the Porterbuddy brand. Both Budbee and Instabox continue to operate as individual services under the Instabee parent company.
Budbee.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Budbee. It is a legitimate Nordic logistics technology company with a focus on sustainable delivery using bikes and electric vehicles. GDPR compliance is expected given its Stockholm headquarters.

BuddyPress.history: BuddyPress is a free, open-source WordPress plugin owned by Automattic that enables WordPress sites to function as social networks. It provides user profiles, activity streams, private messaging, friend connections, and group features. The project has roots dating to around 2008 and has been part of the Automattic ecosystem, with 100,000+ active installs. It is used to build community and membership sites on WordPress, offering tight integration with core WordPress user management.
BuddyPress.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for BuddyPress. It is a GPL-licensed, Automattic-maintained WordPress plugin. Being self-hosted, privacy depends on how the site operator configures it. No security incidents specific to BuddyPress have been widely reported.

Bugcrowd.history: Bugcrowd was founded in September 2012 during a flight from Melbourne to Sydney, Australia by Casey Ellis, Chris Raethke, and Sergei Belokamen. The company pioneered the crowdsourced bug bounty model, connecting organizations with a community of security researchers. It relocated its main headquarters to San Francisco while maintaining offices in Sydney and London. Bugcrowd raised $78.7 million over 6 funding rounds, and in February 2024 raised $102 million with a network of over 500,000 security researchers. Notable clients include OpenAI and US government agencies.
Bugcrowd.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for Bugcrowd. The company is a positive force in the security ecosystem, running legitimate bug bounty programs. By definition it helps organizations improve their security posture through coordinated vulnerability disclosure.

BugHerd.history: BugHerd was co-founded in 2010 by Alan Downie and Matt Milosavljevic in Melbourne, Australia. The platform provides visual website feedback and bug tracking, allowing users to leave annotated comments directly on a live website. BugHerd participated in 500 Startups and Startmate, raising $500,000 in 2012 and $1 million in 2014. In 2018, after a restructuring, Stephen Neville became CEO and partnered with co-founder Alan Downie to relaunch the product, achieving record growth in subsequent years. The tool is widely used by web development and marketing agencies for client feedback management.
BugHerd.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for BugHerd. It is a reputable Australian web feedback tool used by agencies and developers. The tool requires a snippet embedded in websites but collects feedback rather than tracking end users personally.

Buglog.history: Launched in 2020 by an independent developer based in Germany, Buglog is a lightweight, browser-based bug reporting and feedback tool. It was built to help web developers and product managers capture visual feedback and technical metadata directly from users.
Buglog.summary_abuse: Buglog is a B2B bug reporting widget embedded on customer websites. It has no consumer-facing hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

BugSnag.history: Bugsnag was founded in September 2012 by James Smith and Simon Maynard in San Francisco, California. The platform launched publicly in early 2013 after a beta period, tracking over 800,000 crashes per day at launch. In September 2013, it raised a $1.4 million seed round. Bugsnag grew to serve major customers including LinkedIn. In April 2021, Bugsnag was acquired by SmartBear, a software development tooling company. Under SmartBear ownership, Bugsnag continues as a full-stack error monitoring and stability management platform covering mobile, web, desktop, and server applications.
BugSnag.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for Bugsnag. It is a reputable error monitoring platform used by major technology companies. The tool collects application error data and stack traces, but by nature does not track end users personally. SOC 2 Type 2 certified.

Bugzilla.history: Bugzilla was released as open source on August 26, 1998, originally developed by Terry Weissman at mozilla.org. It was among the first products released by the Mozilla project and ported to Perl in September 1998, becoming Bugzilla 2.0. The project became widely adopted across the open-source community, used by Apache, Eclipse, Gentoo, KDE, LibreOffice, the Linux Kernel, Red Hat, and SUSE. In 2023, Zarro Boogs Corporation was formed as a non-profit by core developers to manage the Bugzilla project, with the Bugzilla trademark licensed from the Mozilla Foundation.
Bugzilla.summary_abuse: No abuse signals for Bugzilla. It is a foundational open-source bug tracking system with a 25+ year history of use across major open-source projects. Self-hosted deployments mean privacy is entirely controlled by the operator.

Builder.io.history: Builder.io was founded around 2018 in San Francisco, California by Steve Sewell and Brent Locks, who previously worked together at ShopStyle. The company built a visual headless CMS and no-code development platform combining drag-and-drop editing with AI-powered design-to-code capabilities. Builder.io raised $37.2 million in funding from investors including Greylock and Microsoft's venture fund M12. The platform integrates with any front-end framework and allows non-technical teams to edit content without developer involvement while maintaining composable architecture standards.
Builder.io.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Builder.io. It is a well-funded visual development and CMS platform used by enterprise teams. The platform allows creation of content and applications, and while misuse of built content is possible, the platform itself has a clean track record.

Buildertrend.history: Buildertrend was founded in 2006 in Omaha, Nebraska by Dan Houghton, Steve Dugger, and Jeff Dugger. The three co-founders created the platform to modernize residential construction management, providing builders and remodelers with project scheduling, management, customer communication, and financial tools in one software suite. In 2026, Buildertrend celebrated its 20th anniversary. The company has grown to over 700 employees across offices in Omaha and Lincoln, Nebraska, and serves more than 20,000 contractors globally, including powering over half of new home builds in the United States.
Buildertrend.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Buildertrend. It is a 20-year-old construction management software company with a strong track record. The platform is used by tens of thousands of professional contractors and is a recognized leader in the residential construction technology market.

Bullseye.history: Bullseye Locations was founded in 1998 by Joshua Rich and is headquartered in Somerville, New Jersey. The company provides store locator and dealer locator software for brands with physical retail presence, offering location-based marketing solutions, Shopify store locator apps, and comprehensive location management APIs. Bullseye acquired RevBase and ForFile from Longwood Software Inc. to expand its product portfolio. With over 23 years of operation, the company has achieved approximately $1.3 million in revenue with a 12-person team.
Bullseye.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signals for Bullseye Locations. It is a long-standing niche store locator software provider with a straightforward B2B service model. No reports of data misuse or security incidents found.

Bulma.history: Bulma was created in 2016 by Jeremy Thomas (jgthms) as a free open-source CSS framework based on Flexbox. It gained rapid adoption as a lightweight alternative to Bootstrap with no JavaScript dependency. The project is maintained independently by its creator and has accumulated over 48,000 GitHub stars.
Bulma.summary_abuse: Bulma is a pure CSS framework with no server-side component or hosted service; it has no meaningful abuse surface and no documented abuse signal.

Bump.history: Bump.sh is a French SaaS company founded around 2020 that provides API documentation hosting and diff tracking for OpenAPI and AsyncAPI specifications. The platform allows development teams to auto-publish and version their API docs from CI pipelines.
Bump.summary_abuse: Bump.sh is a developer tooling SaaS for API documentation with no significant abuse surface. It does not provide hosting of end-user content or URL shortening. No notable abuse incidents documented.

bun.history: Bun was created by Jarred Sumner and first publicly released in 2021 as a fast all-in-one JavaScript runtime built on JavaScriptCore. Oven Inc. was founded to support the project. In December 2025, Anthropic acquired Bun as Claude Code reached $1 billion in revenue, making Bun a subsidiary of Anthropic.
bun.summary_abuse: Bun is a JavaScript runtime with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer tool that runs on servers and developers' machines, with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Bunny.history: Bunny.net (originally BunnyCDN) was founded in Slovenia around 2017. What began as a low-cost CDN grew into a multi-product edge platform encompassing CDN, object storage, video streaming, DNS, and image optimization services. The company is bootstrapped and profitable with 119+ PoPs globally.
Bunny.summary_abuse: Bunny.net is a CDN and edge infrastructure provider. Like all CDNs it can be used to cache and deliver malicious content, but the company publishes an AUP and abuse reporting mechanism. No outsized abuse signal compared to other mainstream CDNs.

Bunny Fonts.history: Bunny Fonts was launched by bunny.net in 2022 as a privacy-first drop-in replacement for Google Fonts. It serves fonts with a zero-logging policy and no user tracking, and is fully GDPR-compliant. The service is free, requires no account, and mirrors the Google Fonts API.
Bunny Fonts.summary_abuse: Bunny Fonts is a free font CDN with zero logging and no user tracking by design. It has no meaningful abuse surface and no documented abuse incidents.

Bunting.history: Bunting was founded in the UK around 2013 as a website personalization and A/B testing platform for e-commerce. The company integrates with platforms like Shopware and provides behavioral targeting and product recommendations.
Bunting.summary_abuse: Bunting is a personalization and A/B testing SaaS. As a behavioural analytics layer it collects visitor data for targeting purposes, which introduces typical ad-tech-adjacent privacy concerns, but no notable abuse incidents are documented.

BUROGU.history: BUROGU is a no-code blog creation platform launched around 2022. Limited public history available.
BUROGU.summary_abuse: BUROGU is a blog creation platform. No-code blogging tools can be used to create scam or spam blogs, though there are no documented specific abuse incidents for this platform.

Burst.history: Burst Statistics is a privacy-first website analytics plugin primarily for WordPress, keeping all data on the user's own server to comply with GDPR and other privacy laws. Over 100,000 users have adopted it as an alternative to Google Analytics.
Burst.summary_abuse: Burst Statistics is a self-hosted analytics plugin with no meaningful external abuse surface. Data stays on the site owner's server and the tool has no documented abuse incidents.

Busify.history: Busify is a niche SaaS platform for bus operators to issue tickets, manage fleets, and enhance customer experiences. The company targets small to mid-size transport operators.
Busify.summary_abuse: Busify is a vertical SaaS for bus fleet and ticketing management. It serves a specialized transport operator market and has no documented abuse incidents.

Business Catalyst.history: Adobe Business Catalyst was a cloud-hosted CMS and e-commerce platform originally developed by Australians Adam Broadway and Glen Sherrat around 2004. Adobe acquired it in 2009. Adobe announced end of development on March 26, 2018 and fully shut down the service on March 26, 2021, forcing all customers to migrate.
Business Catalyst.summary_abuse: Business Catalyst was a legitimate Adobe product that has been fully shut down since 2021. No ongoing abuse surface exists as the service is no longer operational.

Business Website Builder.history: Amex Express Checkout is American Express's one-click checkout product for merchants, enabling AmEx cardholders to complete purchases using stored credentials without re-entering card details. It is the AmEx equivalent to PayPal Express and Visa Checkout. The product was developed as part of AmEx's digital payment strategy to reduce checkout friction on e-commerce sites.
Business Website Builder.summary_abuse: Amex Express Checkout is a payment button embedded in merchant checkout flows. It processes AmEx card transactions and is subject to standard payment fraud risks such as stolen card credentials. American Express's fraud detection systems monitor transactions. No specific abuse patterns are documented beyond standard payment fraud.

ButterCMS.history: ButterCMS was founded in 2014 by Jake Lumetta and Abi Noda in Chicago as a headless CMS and blogging API for developers. In December 2022 it was acquired by Tiugo Technologies, a company that acquires and operates developer tools.
ButterCMS.summary_abuse: ButterCMS is a headless CMS API used by developers. Its API-first architecture means there is no public-facing hosting of arbitrary user content, which limits abuse potential. No notable abuse incidents are documented.

ButterMove.history: Founded in 2016 by Bob Caldwell, Chuck Elias, Chris Colleran and Kristi Colleran, 42Chat was originally named Sciensio. The company rebranded to 42Chat as it expanded its focus on curated AI chatbots for events and community organizations. Based in Draper, Utah, it has 14 employees as of 2024.
ButterMove.summary_abuse: 42Chat is a B2B chatbot platform serving event organizers and community-based organizations. No documented abuse history. The platform is not a general-purpose hosting tool and requires business contracts, significantly limiting abuse potential.

Buttonizer.history: Buttonizer is a WordPress plugin and SaaS from the Netherlands that allows website owners to add custom floating call-to-action buttons, including WhatsApp and phone links. It operates as a freemium WordPress plugin with a premium cloud dashboard.
Buttonizer.summary_abuse: Buttonizer is a WordPress CTA button plugin. It has no significant abuse surface and no documented abuse incidents.

Buy me a coffee.history: Buy Me a Coffee was founded in 2017 by two brothers, Jijo Sunny and Vignesh M, as a simple tipping platform for creators. The platform grew to serve over 1 million creators and received backing from Y Combinator. It competes with Ko-fi and Patreon in the creator monetization space.
Buy me a coffee.summary_abuse: Buy Me a Coffee is a creator tipping and membership platform. The open signup model and embedded payment widgets could theoretically be used to solicit payments under false pretenses.
