However, the platform uses Stripe for payments and maintains terms of service prohibiting fraudulent use. No major documented abuse patterns are specific to this platform.

Buy with Prime.history: Buy with Prime was launched by Amazon in April 2022 as a service allowing e-commerce merchants outside Amazon's marketplace to offer Prime shopping benefits — including fast delivery, easy returns, and Amazon Pay — on their own websites. It was initially invite-only and opened broadly to US merchants in 2023.
Buy with Prime.summary_abuse: Buy with Prime is an Amazon service embedded in merchant sites. As a payment and logistics service backed by Amazon's fraud detection, it carries minimal abuse risk in its intended deployment. The Amazon Prime integration means merchants must go through Amazon's onboarding process. No notable abuse incidents documented.

Buyapowa.history: Buyapowa was founded in 2013 in London as a referral marketing and group-buying platform for enterprise brands. The platform enables businesses to run structured referral and advocacy programs across multiple industries.
Buyapowa.summary_abuse: Buyapowa is a B2B referral marketing platform used by enterprises. Referral programs can be exploited by bad actors generating fake referrals for rewards, but Buyapowa's enterprise-only model and brand-level controls limit this risk. No major abuse incidents documented.

Buyee.history: Buyee is a Japanese proxy shopping service operated by Tenso Inc. that allows international buyers to purchase items from Japanese online marketplaces such as Yahoo! Auctions Japan, Mercari, and Rakuten. It was established as one of the first major English-language proxy buying services for Japan.
Buyee.summary_abuse: Buyee is a proxy purchasing service for Japanese e-commerce. As a third-party intermediary, it can be used to circumvent region restrictions or purchase items from sellers who would not otherwise ship internationally. Some counterfeit goods circulate on Japanese auction platforms and Buyee facilitates their international sale, though the company enforces terms of service prohibiting prohibited items. Moderate risk compared to direct marketplace usage.

Buyist.history: Buyist is a code-less e-commerce platform targeting performance marketers. Specific founding details are not prominently documented.
Buyist.summary_abuse: Buyist is a code-less e-commerce builder primarily used by performance marketers. No documented abuse incidents of note; risk level is consistent with typical e-commerce builder platforms.

BuySafe.history: BuySafe is a trust and security seal service for online retailers, providing shoppers with visible signals that a website meets certain safety standards. It offers identity verification and transaction security features.
BuySafe.summary_abuse: BuySafe is a trust-seal product for e-commerce sites. Its trust seal can theoretically be copied or misrepresented on fraudulent sites, but the seal verification links back to BuySafe's records. No major documented abuse incidents.

BuySellAds.history: BuySellAds was founded in 2008 by Nathan Wong and Todd Garland in Boston, Massachusetts as a marketplace directly connecting publishers and advertisers. The company acquired several ad networks including Carbon Ads, Fusion Ads, Beacon Ads, and LaunchBit (2014). It remains bootstrapped.
BuySellAds.summary_abuse: BuySellAds is an advertising marketplace that connects publishers with advertisers. Like all ad networks, it can be a vector for malvertising if ad content is not carefully vetted. The company operates a curated marketplace focused on developer and tech audiences, which reduces but does not eliminate abuse risk. No specific large-scale abuse incidents have been publicly reported.

Buz Club.history: Buz Club is a private club management software platform offering real-time integration for membership, point of sale, and event management at private clubs.
Buz Club.summary_abuse: Buz Club is a vertical CRM for private clubs. It has no significant public-facing abuse surface.

BuzzBuilder.history: BuzzBuilder is an outbound sales automation platform that orchestrates cold email sequences, LinkedIn outreach, and follow-up calls for B2B lead generation. It targets SMB sales teams.
BuzzBuilder.summary_abuse: BuzzBuilder is an automated outbound email and LinkedIn engagement tool used for cold prospecting. Mass-email platforms carry inherent spam risk; bulk cold outreach can violate CAN-SPAM or GDPR if recipients have not opted in. No specific large-scale abuse incidents involving BuzzBuilder are documented, but the tool category is frequently implicated in unsolicited commercial email.

bxSlider.history: bxSlider is a responsive jQuery content slider created around 2012. It became widely used for image carousels and slideshows on websites. Activity has slowed significantly in recent years.
bxSlider.summary_abuse: bxSlider is a JavaScript slider library with no notable abuse signal. It is a pure frontend UI component with no server-side surface.

Bynder.history: Bynder was founded in 2013 in Amsterdam by Chris Hall, Roland Keijzer, and Stefan Pelders, who identified the need for a cloud-based digital asset management platform while working at a software agency. The company grew 350% year-on-year in its first three years and raised a $22.2M Series A from Insight Partners in 2016. It acquired Shutterstock's WebDam DAM business for $49.1M in 2018, We Adapt in 2020, and GatherContent in 2022. In late 2022, Thomas H. Lee Partners acquired Insight Partners' stake at a $600M valuation.
Bynder.summary_abuse: Bynder is an enterprise digital asset management platform serving marketing teams at large brands. It has no meaningful public-facing abuse surface; content is stored by contracted enterprise customers. No documented abuse incidents.

Byscuit.history: Byscuit is a Canadian cookie consent management platform focusing on GDPR and Quebec Law 25 compliance. The company hosts all data in Canada and provides bilingual English/French support.
Byscuit.summary_abuse: Byscuit is a consent management platform (CMP) with no meaningful abuse surface. Its function is to collect and store user consent records. No documented abuse incidents.

BySide.history: BySide is a Portuguese marketing automation and personalization platform founded around 2006. It provides dynamic content personalization, behavioral targeting, and real-time web engagement for enterprises.
BySide.summary_abuse: BySide is a B2B marketing automation and personalization tool. As an analytics and targeting layer it processes visitor behavioral data but serves enterprise clients under contract. No notable abuse incidents documented.
